properties.Description: The Bicentennial Bikeway is the best. Separated not only from cars, but also separated space for pedestrians. Beautiful views along the river. This is about the only place in Brisbane I can cycle without any stress at all.

Safe for the marked lanes for walkers and riders, lots of use and views of town. Also smooth concrete to roll on and be separated from cars. Just need to have plenty of sunscreen applied & sunnies on as there is absolutely no shade (on white radiant concrete next to the reflecting river)
Safer bridge to use than Kurilpa as it has marked space for walkers and riders. Also usually has lighting, and is partially shaded by the awning.
Separated Bike lane though the CBD- the safest and quickest way to get from one side to the other. Currently closed near the Victoria Bridge.

Fantastic shared path with a good volume of cyclists (especially during peak times). Although this is a shared path, it's wide enough that you can safely hold a reasonable speed on the bike (that makes cycling a realistic method of transport).

There are some road crossings, but traffic must give way and I'm yet to have a close call with a vehicle.

The Riverwalk is fantastic. I look forward to reaching this section on my commute. Smooth, well marked separated path designed so that pedestrians are happy to stay on the footpath section- river view and seats for them
This bike path is long and well away from cars. I ride there a lot because it is safe.
Lights and other users at most times of the day
The is a great bit of infrastructure
Lighting, people using the space all times of day, dedicated facilities and places to visit
The V1 is a great facility. Pretty wide and most importantly, separated from cars,
The V1 is an excellent route that supports high speed bidirectional bike traffic. There are few obstacles, no vehicles and generally no pedestrians. It's busy in peak times.
To be fair, the Enoggera Cr bikeway is great - rubbish connections on either end, but the actual bikeway is pretty good.

Off-road separated path for people walking and riding. Good shade from big trees, views of town, as some night-lights, water stops. Great place to roll through and mostly flat. Entry from Hill End Terrace is a bit tight/narrow, otherwise good.

The Logan Road underpass finally opened earlier this year, creating a safe route for cyclists, pedestrians etc to cross Logan Rd. The map makes the bike path appear to intersect Logan Rd but it actually passes safely underneath. This is a separated facility for pedestrians and bikes.
The Centenary Bikeway from Darra to Toowong is brilliant, especially the new underpass. Fixing the missing links to the bikeway would make a huge difference.
dedicated and separated cycle lane.  it would be great if there was more infrastructure like this in the Brisbane area.

Sir William MacGregor Dr is a fairly safe place to ride on-road. Lots of bikes, low speed limit, speed bumps, drivers are generally considerate. The road is lined with parked cars during the day, there is a risk of being car-doored mainly at ~7-9am and ~4-6pm. Most cars belong to commuters and there is little parked car turnover during the day.
Feel safe as separated facilities with great views. However, the footpath is not wide enough for social walking so people often expand into the bike lanes.

properties.Location: Boggo Road Bridge Access, Woolloongabba, Queensland
properties.Description: PA bike bridge is excellent. (Crossing Ipswich Rd was a nightmare). Boggo Rd connection can't come soon enough to avoid the nightmare that is Annerley Rd/Noble St/Cornwall St junction

The CityLink cycleway has massively improved the safety and reduced the stress of cycling through the CBD. The bike path is wide, bi-directional and barrier/grade separated from both vehicle and pedestrian traffic. Providing a safe place for bikes and scooters has also enhanced safety of pedestrians and utility of the footpath.
Kedron Brook bikeway is well made and nicely separated from traffic. It makes it much easier to get to places along it's path.
Great bike path along the creek. Wide and good visibility.
Reasonably wide path, well used.

properties.Main Safe Reason: lots of other bike riders here
properties.Description: West End River park has dedicated cycling path with enough space for both walkers and cyclists.

properties.Location: 65 Gardens Point Road, Brisbane City, Queensland
properties.Description: This is the easiest to navigate of all the bridge on/off ramps in the Brisbane CBD area. There is plenty of space here, there is good signage, and good visibility of and connectivity to paths travelling south, east and west. There are often many hire scooters and bikes here, but there is enough space so they are not a hazard.
As a cyclist, crossing Moggill Road was a nightmare before this bridge was built, fantastic solution for cycling and vehicle traffic is less interupted too..
This is one of the best bits of cycle infrastructure in Brisbane
Dedicated away from cars space although too many pedestrians can cause worry
Dedicated space for people riding, great views, separated from cars by a big concrete barrier. Could be a wider for people walking/fishing
I like the way the design of the ferry terminal allows walkers to get to the lights at Coronation drive without having to cross the bike path (looking at you Queens Wharf)
Nice pleasant off road bikeway. Well used by people walking and riding. Could be wider though.
On a path with good separation (30m) away from the busy road. I am amazed other cyclists persist on the road. I think some don't see that the path exists.

properties.Location: 345 Kelvin Grove Road, Kelvin Grove, Queensland
properties.Description: The underpass at Kelvin Grove Rd is a critical part of the Enoggera Cr bike way and makes the whole thing work compared to having cross this major road.  Compare with the situation closer to the city where you are dumped onto Bowen Bridge Rd with no underpass and must negotiate multiple crossings
Hoorah for free bicycle crossings. Thanks to the councils and company responsible. Its appreciated.
Nice wide seperate bike and pedestrian path along the Kedron Brook. The exits can be quite steep but the bikeway itself is pretty flat.
This is a great example of how cities can create active transport infrastructure that is a viable alternative to driving a car.

This is a sneaky little bridge that lets you cross the railway line without having to use Sandgate road.

It's not great. It's very narrow and has a few stairs so you need to dismount, but at least it's away from traffic.

properties.Location: 87a Marshall Road, Holland Park West, Queensland
properties.Description: This new path connecting to the V1 is excellent.
Well lit crossing point of busy road with a contained/fenced refuge island.
V1 should be designed as per this section which is buffered away from the M1 vehicular traffic, less noise, fume, it should also increase shade opportunities as this is bare how no weather protection
Great bike path separated from the road

This is a pleasant ride away from car noise and a great connection to the V1 despite being a little hilly. It is a little narrow however which makes passing other path users a little hazardous, but beats riding on roads.
The actual path is good, but it's right next to the motorway and particularly noisy at this section.
This is a great bikepath through a quiet and picturesque area.
It is a quiet area, with not many cars

properties.Location: Mount Glorious Road, Mount Nebo, Queensland
properties.Description: The best way to get to Mt Nebo or Mt Glorious. Can be rutted at times, but nice winding road, with little traffic, wide road, and one way.

All these roads along here aren't connected except for bicycle/pedestrian, so the only cars here aren't proceeding 'through.' This slows them down, and there aren't many of them, either. The route in general is pretty safe.

It's safe, broad, shaded, and pretty good for the length that it runs. Shame that it kind of fades away into driveways and then disappears completely by Hawthorne, but it is good for connecting to the Normanby Bikeway, and from there to points South.
way from cars

properties.Location: 41 Craig Street, Eight Mile Plains, Queensland
properties.Description: This would have to be one of the most pleasant places in Brisbane - green, tranquil, shaded, easy riding!
Pretty safe, all things considered. Norman Ave. could use with a bike lane or removing on-street parking so it would connect the other path a bit better.

properties.Location: South Boundary Road, Enoggera Reservoir, Queensland
properties.Description: It's out in the bush, free from any vehicles.
The only safe, separated bike lanes on Moggill Road exist in this small stretch outside the recently completed Moggill Village shopping centre.

Before the bridge over Slacks Creek was built, the only way for pedestrians and cyclists to go up or down Crompton Rd. was to ride or walk amongst the traffic. (No verge at all) Although it only took over 50 years to build, I am grateful that it finally got done. (Hopefully the Nygaard Park path between Park Rd. and Moss St. won't take as long.)
No cars. Lovely sealed tracks for cycling
This is a safe railway crossing for riders, including people who don't feel safe riding in traffic. The footpath is wide and usually there aren't many pedestrians here.
Off road bike path.

Arthur Street is by far the best way to commute from Newstead/Teneriffe to the city. The road is wide, the traffic is low and it's often frequented by cyclists. You do have to watch out for dooring though.
Plenty of room on the road for drivers to pass without being too close.

A bicycle lane that actually works! It allows you to travel against the traffic, completely separated. If only the rest of the city had these...
I use this and then George St because the Edward St bike lanes are perpetually *RED* because they prioritise turning car traffic.
Great bike lane, well seperated with large safety barrier. Bikeway can take you all teh way to Nynda with miniumal interaction with cars and road traffic
Great new cycle path away from Lomandra Drive

Wide separated lane from people in cars and pedestrians. Enhanced by vegetation (clear separation from people walking) and interest with narrow shopfront and quaint bars to look into as rolling by. I appreciate the visual interest, and safe in the separated lane to do so.
The underpass for Jubilee Tce is a critical part of the Ithaca Ck bikeway, avoiding having to cross Jubilee Tce
Wide bicycle path with a decent surface

Having a dedicated cycle/pedestrian bridge in a suburb this far from the city centre feels like it doesn't get the recognition it deserves. once i reach this bridge I know the rest of my ride will be relatively stress free. Unfortunately most of riding south of this bridge is not as good.
V1 is great infrastructure - particularly the newer sections.

This is a pleasant meandering 'flow' through the Boondall Wetlands. It can get a bit puddly after the rain, and sometimes the mosquitos can get nasty, but overall it is a flat lovely ride through some interesting wetland scenery under the open sky. A real gem. Hop on your bike/feet/etc and go there. Continue up to Sandgate, or head to the city.

properties.Location: Eleanor Schonell Bridge, St Lucia, Queensland
properties.Description: Overall it's pretty swell.
no traffic

Moreton Bay Cycleway is a great piece of infrastructure for Redcliffe, excellent for recreational rides or commutes. If I were to complain I'd only say that having more sections separate from pedestrian paths would be better, there can be some conflict on busy weekends.
This part of the bikeway is on the other side of a sound barrier so it's quiet and pleasant to ride along
the timing of these lights is quite good. Hideous road to cross at any other point but i'm pleased that the beg button is easy to access and the wait to cross is relatively short

properties.Location: 43 Thomas Macleod Avenue, Sinnamon Park, Queensland
properties.Description: Lovely, safe shared path along the river between Jindalee and Rocks Riverside Park. Well used by cyclists and pedestrians. Generally very well behaved and considerate users of all types.
Safe pathway that works well enough. It's very wide, wide enough even for when people are walking abreast there's plenty of space to go around.
Desgrand Street is a great alternate from the LGIP Primary Cycle route, Beaudesert Rd. Link Boniface to Desgrand, Kerry, to Postle St for a safe route to Mortimer Rd at Acacia Ridge.

In general the cars will give you plenty of room or yield to your descent. I haven't had any ugly incidents on this road, which is far better to come down than Gladstone Road, (though it's far less direct than Gladstone)
Better alternate route to Kessels/Riawena Rd due to much less traffic. Though the straight and wide road does encourage speeding drivers and there's no bike-specific infrastructure along this road.
Kerry Road is a good alternate from the LGIP Primary Cycle route, Beaudesert Rd. Link Boniface to Desgrand, Kerry, to Postle St for a safe route to Mortimer Rd at Acacia Ridge
Pleasant path to ride along

properties.Location: 355a Sir Fred Schonell Drive, St Lucia, Queensland
properties.Description: This roundabout and the roads it connects are all safe places to ride. There are traffic calming measures and cars let me join the loop and don't drive aggressively here.
Devoted bicycle path - wonderful
Pretty good bike lane here although sometimes encounter cars parked in the lane.
road furniture cut back when upgrades done,  no longer a squeeze point for bikes on shoulder
road furniture cut back when upgrades done,  no longer a squeeze point for bikes on shoulder
separation, plenty of riders, and feels overall pleasant

properties.Location: 10 Little Bennett Street, Norman Park, Queensland
properties.Description: cars can't travel from the roundabout onto Agnew Street to the west, but bikes can.  Creates a quiet back street to cycle on
Low traffic, good route to take avoiding some of the busier roads
Safe because its slow traffic and many cyclists commute by here. Could be improved by removing car door-hazards for people on bikes.
Annerley Road is pretty safe overall, they've done good work with the improvements.
It's a nice off-road path with good connectivity options
Safe high-speed bike corridor
The Coopers Camp Rd underpass is a critical part of the Ithaca Ck bikeway, avoiding having to cross Coopers Camp Rd
Well populated, plenty of room for cyclists and walkers.
Separated bike lane is great but would be fantastic is the protected bike lane connected all the way to kingsford smith drive
The 10 km/h speed limit is "advisory" and is not enforceable. Any tickets written will be overturned. Goodwill Bridge's limit, however, is not 'advisory.'
Very wide path so when stadium events are not on it makes a good connection from the Bicentennial Bikeway to Paddington

An ok connection between Bulimba Creek Bikeway (Brisbane) and Rickertt Road (Redlands Coast), Using Meadowlands Road path, Belmont Road path, cross refuge island to Hillcroft Place, Summit Street, highland cres, left onto walkway, Dairy swamp road, Formosa Rd, Judd Street, Feathertail Place, Habitat Dr Park, Habitat Dr, Green Camp Road, Rickertt RD
Dirt path which could do with paving. Good link from Tarragindi Road to Tarragindi Road.
This is a nice alley to ride through, much better than Grey St. it's fairly narrow so traffic speeds are low, the only trouble is nothing else really connects up to it.
Drivers are usually pretty good on Camford and Kilroe Streets. Possible to get through on the footpath although the path width varies a lot.
nice wide on road cycle lane running the length of Lyndon road
part of a great safe trail from Toowong to Dinmore

this is an area that beginners can feel comfortable to get a start on riding. the circuit is small but it is good to build confidence for those who are starting out or restarting a new adventure riding
Wide roads
Wide bike lane, I always feel safe  riding down this road

properties.Location: 18 Mclean Road South, Camp Mountain, Queensland
properties.Description: Whereas there is still opportunity for a seperate bike lane, as this is a frequent favourite, the drivers are almost always courteous and patient (not you Brisbane Buslines). As you approach Petersen there is almost zero cars.
Great new wide cycleway that runs alongside a reasonably quiet road
Dedicated cycleway
Dedicated cycleway

properties.Location: 4a Musgrave Street, Fig Tree Pocket, Queensland
properties.Description: Separated bikeway
Easy safe access to the Centenary Cycleway away from Yallambee Rd
Dedicated tunnel under highway, now with working lights
Only threat out here's Maggies, and the ones nesting in this park area are pretty chill.
More separated lanes please
Great upgrade made to this bike path intersection in 2023.  Wider, less grade, better visibility.

Safe during the day as it is wide and rolling concrete path. Unsafe if not travelling if not travelling with a companion or at night as it has limited passive surveillance (CPTED), hidden by beautiful bush reserves if in any trouble. Every time I've used it, it only has a few others using it.
Good lighting, no traffic, relatively many people.
Care is required due to narrow footpath with blind corners, however the tunnels going under the intersection means you don't need to cross any lanes of traffic.
Wide path separated from the road. Perfect!
Self locking gate allowing easy cycle access to bushland
Brilliant bikeway to get to Brushbox Ct and Chapel Hill state school. Nice and wide, but likely to flood during heavy rain.
A great spot for kids (and bigger kids) to ride a 500 metre circuit
A flat, pleasant ride on a shared cycle/pedestrian path along the creek. We rode from Virginia station to Chermside shops. There was signage along the path but it wasn't always clear.
Good spot for kids to ride bikes on the waterfront/ wide path
There's no obstacles or other people, water levels are rarely more than millimetres deep

properties.Location: Rocks Riverside Park, Seventeen Mile Rocks, Queensland
properties.Description: Off road cycleway
Generally slow traffic.
Nice wide path, can get busy at school times and when lots of kids at the park but usually pretty good.
Boniface Street is a great alternate from the LGIP Primary Cycle route, Beaudesert Rd. Link Boniface to Desgrand, Kerry, to Postle St for a safe route to Mortimer Rd at Acacia Ridge.

It is wonderful to ride through here and see kids riding bikes, scooters and playing on the street, and families walking and adults jogging. This is the benefit of filtered permeability - it's not a through street, so it's a place people can enjoy the street as a place, not a thoroughfare.
My main concern is cycling across Story Bridge. The options are the pedestrian lane or the bridge pavement with 6 car lanes. A dedicated bike lane is desirable.

An ok backstreet option that is quicker than the KBB connecting Ferny Grove & Everton Park. Claverton St, Avington St, Annandale St, Jarrah St, Sivertop St, Dallas Pde, Tully St, Kane St, cross Dawson Pde, Blaker St, Glen Holm St, University Rd, Grovely Tce, Blackwood St, McConaghy St Park, McConaghy St, Burwood St path (to Brookside Park & KBB)
Council replaced 2 offset cross barriers with one upright barrier.  Good job.

Safe off road path with good space from people walking. Nice views of the bay, people swimming, intermittent shade and toilets/water access. Finishes at a set of lights to cross road. Easy. Only have to watch people walking from beach to cars.

properties.Main Safe Reason: lots of other bike riders here
properties.Description: Lovely to see so many people riding, kids on trikes, kids playing, some older persons in a bike group - joggers, pram users, commuters with kids on back of bikes.  A very diverse mix of users.
families use this road to drop off children to childcare centres
The 10km/h speed limit is not safe for cyclists to do on a dedicated cycle route
The North Brisbane Bikeway just ends at Price Street. Brisbane City Council has failed for 6 years to follow through with a design. Absolutely appalling negligence.

This is the end of the very popular Western Freeway Bikeway inbound. Cyclists must cross Dean Street via a bike crossing to get to to Sylvan Rd via bus depot and Miskin St. However visibility is hampered by large trees and parked cars <100m. Large truck has been parked. Bikes must enter road, avoiding outbound bikes to see right! DANDEROUS!!!
The shared path ends you then have to enter the on road bike lane that regularly has parked cars

This is the main connection between the Centenary Bikeway and Indooroopilly Riverwalk, heavily used by school and university students, and yet there is no intersection, and a dangerous, offset intersection. Council needs to hurry up and build something here.
Lots of traffic to cross making it unsafe for cyclists
As above, trucks passing very close which can be intimidating.

Council installed bike only lights and then allows vehicles to turn left over the bike lane.  If they removed one sequence from the cycle it would be much safer (don't allow trough traffic while vehicles can turn left on to Patrick lane)
Cars will cut off bicycle riders trying to get to this intersection first

properties.Location: New Farm Riverwalk, Brisbane City, Queensland
properties.Description: This "shared zone" forces 2 lanes of cars, delivery trucks, walkers, & cyclists together at a narrow pinchpoint. On a Fri or Sat night, cars are jammed bumber to bumper, and pedestrians, cycles, & scooters are forced between moving cars, or up against the service wall of the restaurants. There are multiple conflict zones.
This corner into and out of Hawthorne road is narrow, shared, blind spot, often has scooters lying near it, and frequently used.  Terrible.
bikes squeezed into path of cars at roundabout

Herschel Street/North Quay intersection is compromised by construction going on in the street. Footpath and far left line often closed. Extremely dangerous to get to and from the Bicentennial Bikeway.
Part of a circuit route and a corridor for bike commuters, constant traffic either dropping off at the schools and/or travelling at high speed
The bike and pedestrian bridge just ends and dumps you in the middle of the city with very poor cycling safety infrastructure.

properties.Location: 310a Fig Tree Pocket Road, Fig Tree Pocket, Queensland
properties.Description: Dedicated bike lane ends then you have to cross the motorway on and off ramps
Need a signed/dedicated way to get from Parkland Blvd to the Tank St/Kurilpa Bridge Bikeway

A main commuting route to the city - crosses over Archer St. Vehicles do not look / are unaware of speed. This crossing should be raised, a zebra crossing or vehicle free. There are frequent incidents.
A five way active travel intersection with limited to no visibility - this intersection is a disaster. It is used by commuting cyclists, scooters, runners, pedestrians and tourists.

Zero infrastructure to continue north from here to the Valley.
Many of the footpaths along Turbot St are very narrow to be riding with pedestrians.

High pedestrian traffic, 40 zone with paved road surface, speed bumps and cars parked down the left. Drivers will aggressively tailgate and try to pass cyclists already doing the 40km/hr limit despite a very narrow road and high risk environment. This road should honestly be blocked completely to through car traffic.

properties.Location: New Farm Riverwalk, Brisbane City, Queensland
properties.Description: Cars and trucks are regularly parked in this No Standing zone that is designated as a pedestrian and bicycle zone by the coloured pavement. This narrows the Shared Zone down to a single one-way lane, forcing bicycles and pedestrians into oncoming traffic.
Car drivers will deliberately speed up to pass bicycle riders, then cut them off at Bennett St.
Once the bike way ends at Kippa-Ring station there is no safe direct  bike path down to the Ted Smout Bridge bikeway
The sign says 'cross with care' - the pedestrian signal should be left green given it is on the suggested commuting route to the city / Uni of Qld
This is the only link between The Gap and Keperra, but is car only. High speed cars, lots of traffic, narrow.  Mad to ride it.

The 500 metres along Dickson Street from Price to Keith Streets is the only missing link in a safe bikeway from the CBD to Redcliffe. It is a dangerous section of door zone with fast, heavy traffic at peak times. It is a barrier to all but confident riders. This means less active transport use, and more traffic congestion.
terrible crossing for bikes trying to get across Kate St  from Witton rd to Indooroopilly riverwalk

properties.Location: 50 Lower River Terrace, South Brisbane, Queensland
properties.Description: Those downhill section of the bikeway I feel is dangerous and narrow with a steep downhill. I know Brisbane council and QLD state government have looked at an easier transfer but seems that's not proceeding ahead. So does it take a fatality to get things fixed
All of Lambert Rd (with four schools and associated traffic within 500m, and a main route to Uni of Qld) is narrow, very busy and very dangerous to school children riding to school / commuters.

Phil Pawsey was struck and killed here. Brisbane City Council has been warned repeatedly that Nudgee Road is unsafe without separated bike lanes, but have refused to act. Even now they refuse to act until the coroner tells them to.
Lack of shoulder and a narrow lane for even cars in both directions has resulted in this being a hotspot for dangerous overtaking and high risk for cycling commuters
No footpath access across railway crossing, very narrow two-lane stop zone and high frequency of trucking traffic has made this spot very hostile for cycling, walking (or even P.W.D) commuters
Cars existing from hidden driveways on southern side cannot easily see cyclists without entering the road.

Ridiculous that this important piece of cycling infrastructure can be completely closed, for multiple years, without providing an adequate and safe diversion.     Battling traffic on Mary, Creek and Eagle Streets is a nightmare.   And someone will get seriously hurt.
Off ramp for cars prioritised over people riding/walking at corner of school. Very dangerous speeds.
Trying to ride Nudgee road is chaotic and dangerous. Cars are far too close, the bike lane is constantly blocked and cars parked in the bike lane impacts visibility and safety.

properties.Location: 2277c Logan Road, Eight Mile Plains, Queensland
properties.Description: This has to be the most dangerous single lane road crossing anywhere in Brisbane. Cars come screaming down the hill on Logan Road and onto the motorway on ramp, often without signalling. Due to the high volume of traffic, it is nearly impossible to cross here without taking a risk.

For some unknown reason, council decided to put in a footpath entry here that does a really awkward dog leg that is difficult to negotiate.  this meansthat kids cannot easily use the footpath to ride to school and Twigg st is a nightmare.  This corner needs re-making for a smooth entry and crossing straight off the riverwalk.
The bicycle lane ends with no alternative for travelling east-west

It's dangerous when heading northbound towards the brekkie creek bridge, you're forced to navigate a dangerous set of roads whilst hoping no one blocks the ramp up to the path, or a car doesn't suddenly appear.

Hopefully this is a moot point once the green bridge comes in
Drivers turning/ cars in bike lane / busy area
Needs protected lanes in both directions between the end of the protected lane on the CityLink and Centenary Place park at the top of the Valley.

This is a stupid car-centric design that ignores the need for cyclists to turn right off Witton rd into Bridge st with the footpath forcing bicycles to ride the wrong way down a oneway section of road in the middleof a school dropzone.  There is no safe right turn and yet cyclists are expected to somehow access Twigg st to get onto the riverwalk?
Fast heavy traffic, large trucks, no bike lane
Poor Bike Connection to Kingfordsmith Road cycle way

Oncoming traffic turn right into the STARS hospital across backed up traffic. Most of the time the cyclist is unable to see these cars meaning the cyclist will either crash into the car or brake rapidly and narrowly miss the car

Busstop squeezes out bicycles into the traffic and createsa dangerous situation.  The road is narrow especially during school drop times because cars park on the other side of the road as well, effectively squeezing it down to one narrow lane each way.  The bus stopshould be moved 30m back towards Harts rd to reduce congestion on this intersection.
Unnecessary road crossing required 2 times in 20 meters to ride/cross the train line. Cars get so upset when you follow the actual bike path provided. It is part of the MBC and is very unsafe
Cyclists travel down the right hand side of the one way street so they can turn right into Regatta St. Motorists creep right squeezing the space for cyclists. There is ample room for a bike lane.

properties.Location: 12 Sir Samuel Griffith Drive, Mount Coot-Tha, Queensland
properties.Description: Terrible pinch point between drivers and cyclists on a corner linking one of the most popular (on and off road) cycling areas in Brisbane.

The connection between bicentennial bikeway (city) and Roma st parklands is really poor and confusing in both directions. It would be fantastic to have a safe connection so you can travel from city eg botanical gardens to Roma st and on to RBWH.

Cars driving along Balmoral St move into the parking lane  just after the intersection in preparation of turning left further along. This makes it dangerous when cyclists 
transition from the service station forecourt to the parking lane. A kerb build out protecting cyclists would make priorities clearer and therefore safer.
Fast, heavy traffic and large trucks

A PMD rider can currently legally ride along Sylvan Rd which is a 50kph road with a bike lane. TMR acknowldeges this safe for the PMD rider.
When turning left onto Land St, the road speed increases to 60kph with no means for the PMD rider to safely exit the road.
The 50kph limit when coming from Sylvan Rd should remain until reaching Patrick Lane.

A PMD rider after exiting the Bicentennial Bikeway at Moorlands Park will often cross Land St at the provided crossing zone.
A PMD rider should be able to safely use the bike lane from the crossing up to Sylvan Rd.
To do so, the road speed needs to be reduced to 50kph.

It is a slow area for bikes and cars seem to make bad decisions. They feel they need to pass bikes in the same lane, even though there are 2 lanes and there is simply not enough space to pass safely. There is no bike lane, or even a verge on the road and cars travel fast.

no bike lane. riding in the shoulder along this area can be worse in the line markings. I find most cars and trucks try to speed to get around you while the road closes in which creates an extremely unsafe area to ride

properties.Location: 32 Birdwood Road, Holland Park West, Queensland
properties.Description: Major connector to the V1 veloway with no marked bike lane, car parking free for all in the shoulder, and disappearing shoulders. Drive behaviour is also very poor with frequent close passes and severe aggression. A very dangerous section with zero attempt at improving cyclist safety.
Richard Pollett was struck and killed here in 2011. As yet, nothing has changed to make it safer.
Have witnessed multiple accidents along Sylvan Rd.
Lack of bicycle lane and being the sole connection between two major business/ industrial hubs (Trade Coast & Airport) has resulted in frequent trucking traffic.

properties.Location: 1d Bowen Bridge Road, Bowen Hills, Queensland
properties.Description: No bike lane on Bowen Bridge Rd, footpath is extremely tight with BCC Bus Shelter, Advertising Shells, High Speed & Volume of Car traffic

Cyclists not brave enough to ride on Newmarket Road have to contend with extremely narrow footpaths obstructed by rubbish bins and power line poles. All you can do is ride slow and pray no one is coming the other way. Just don't forget to keep your balance as the wind from traffic tries to blow you off the bike and under a car wheel.
Lots of traffic, lots of parked cars. No bike lane. Short section of north brisbane bikeway that is not complete
Fast heavy traffic and big trucks
Fast moving traffic, no shoulder, tight corners and nowhere for bikes /pedestrians to go

This is part of a crucial missing link between the North Brisbane Bikeway and Kedron Brook. People on bicycles and scooters need to get across fast, busy traffic to make the connection. A crossing light would allow people headed north to reach the safe back roads of Sydney and Jackson Streets to connect with the bikeways at Kalinga Park.
Narrow bridge, tiny footpath not suitable for cycling, no hard shoulder

Cars from Fig Tree Pocket side of the bridge flick up stones and debris off the road into oncoming rider's path on the narrow bridge crossing.  There is no protection to stop flying objects from hitting riders.  My son had a piece of gravel narrowly miss his eye while crossing the bridge there heading towards Fig Tree Pocket.
This is a busy road without a full dedicated bike lane from Coorparoo to Stones Corner.

properties.Location: 15 Glen Ross Road, Sinnamon Park, Queensland
properties.Description: You have to interact with traffic entering or exciting the motorway
No bike lane narrow and busy section

This is an extremely dangerous intersection for people riding bicycles from the North Brisbane Bikeway toward Wellington Street and Kedron Brook. The main motor vehicle traffic is turning left to drive along Chalk Street, and drivers frequently cut off people continuing along Kedron Brook Road on bicycles.

There is always a huge flow of cars coming here. There are no traffic lights to allow cyclists to cross. The longest I have had to wait here is 12 minutes. Honking is common as you have to make do with the small gaps that exist. Drivers are unaware of the problem and behave dangerously.
Enoggera Ck bikeway ends at bridge crossing, with no link to Bowen Bridge Rd, hospital and Fortitude Valley. No bike lane and high volume of traffic

The outbound lane of Wynnum Rd in this stretch is an 80km/h zone but there is no bike lane or even shoulder to ride on. I frequently get deliberate close passes on this stretch. There is a bike path but its connectivity to the outbound direction makes its use completely impractical.

Vulture St is the main access road for West End to basically anywhere on the southside, but there are no bike lanes and the traffic is terrible. I've been aggressively beeped and nearly hit here many times.
Fast, heavy traffic and large trucks

To turn right on to Kingsford Smith Drive you have to be in the middle lane (the left lane goes straight) which means you're in the middle of all the vehicle traffic, and motorists aren't always nice. As a woman, I hate this intersection.
Multidirectional through traffic at this intersection especially at work commute times

Only the right lane can go straight down brookfield road and it is very difficult to get a bike into the right lane at the speeds cars are driving. Most want to go left and there is a lot of traffic here.

properties.Location: New Farm Riverwalk, Brisbane City, Queensland
properties.Description: Shared space is very busy with vehicles dropping off and picking up passengers. The busy path means pedestrians and cyclists often conflict as well
A simple bridge across the creek here would provide a very valuable connection East to West connecting Pallara to Algester. No East/West connection currently exists!
Severe bump in road surface on descent- enough to cause rider to lose grip of bars and crash. (sadly, proven outcome)
Brisbane Road is a vital link between Ipswich CBD and the Ipswich Motorway cycleway towards Brisbane. There is no cycling infrastructure, drivers are extremely hostile, and it's extremely unsafe.
Richard Burden was struck and killed here in 2017. Nothing has changed to improve it since then.
Lomandra Drive has no shoulder and many heavy construction vehicles use this road
Too narrow. Parked cars can door people passing by.

Dangerous intersection with multiple directions of cars to look out for when crossing rail tracks. Can pass over tracks via the train pedestrian crossing but dangerous to then cross back across the to continue down lindum rd.

Absolute horror of an overly wide slipway that encourages motorists to speed into Nudgee Road; add to that traffic filtering from right to left of the two-lanes northbound to avoid right-turning traffic ahead has often resulted in many heart-stopping near misses here!

Bike Lane narrows a lot along this stretch, and you're pinned up against a stone wall with cars that don't really seem to mind their speed or give much room. They are often trucks or large vehicles, and they are moving at at least 60 km/h through this point.

There is a very narrow shoulder up Griffith Road but no official bike lane. It's poorly lit so make sure you're visible and can see if you ride it. There are signs at the top and bottom of the hill advising cyclists not to ride this track and use the adjacent Johnson Path (stairs).
Narrow road, heavy traffic including busses. no bike lane
There is ZERO bike infrastructure in this area. There is no safe way to get between Sherwood Road and Tennyson
Fast heavy traffic especially trucks. Would commute along here regularly, but daren't cycle along this road
Fast heavy traffic, large trucks, no bike lane
Forced by road curb to move into traffic lanes.

From Nadine St to the Pamphlett Bridage this section of Graceville Ave Graceville forms a section of the Brisbane River Loop. Thousands of cyclists use this route each week for commuting Recreation and Fitness Training. The road is just too barrow for safe cyclist and motoring.

Vehicles exit the roundabout at speed and a rider has difficulty assessing whether a vehicle is turning left from the roundabout until the last moment. It is difficult to grab hold of the holding rail - it is badly located and should be designed with an offset top rail as used at the crossing of Sinnamon Road near Jindalee Fitness
Multiple times cars are abusive when you are coming down the hill
There is a complete lack of cycling infrastructure in Stafford and surrounding suburbs.

The bike lane ends as it crosses this road and the signage is poor and inconsistent compared to the road crossings at Grafton, Bowen & Somerset st that the bike line crosses in close proximity. Cyclists cross onto McDonald Rd and leave the bikeway to gain right of way. I have witnessed a cyclist being hit by a car and several near misses here
Crossing the added lane with a large volume of vehicles coming off the M1 is not safe.

It is dangerous because there is a lot of fast cars trying to enter the freeway and they do not want to stop for cyclists to cross. Even when I'm halfway across. At peak times it is a car/cyclist clash spot.
poor signal phasing is confusing and does not protect the crossing where you start from  static or walking

The give way sign is located too far down the slip and out of site of drivers looking for traffic on Main Street. As a result pedestrians, wheelchair users and cyclists pile onto a tiny island during peak hour traffic unless someone steps out and stops traffic, pointing to the five way sign. This results in increased aggressive driver behavior.

Less experienced vehicle drivers really struggle at this intersection - needing to look instantly left, middle and right as well as watching for pedestrians and cyclists. So many near misses during school drops and peak hours. Pedestrian and Bicycle separation needed
Unsafe close pass, aggressive driver behaviour through intersection west bound. Felt life threatening experience from speed and near miss from vehicle.
I have almost been hit at this intersection multiple times. This intersection is unsafe for bicyclists, pedestrian, and cars.
No space for cyclists and impedes the option to commute by bicycle.
No bike lane and no other option but to drive on the road
Fast heavy traffic and large trucks

On the way to the city the road connects into an exceptionally good bikeway, but a rider has to risk crossing the toad to get there. Car dooring is dangerous too, so is traffic volume in the morning during commuting time.
Roundabout intersection with unprotected bike lanes. Most vehicles stop in bike lane to access roundabout.

In the afternoon peak there is a constant stream of cars merging onto Old Cleveland Rd. The speed limit is 80km/h so they are moving fast. The bike lane crosses the merging lane and it is really difficult to get a break long enough to cross safely (not to mention you have to come to a complete stop which is a bit waste of energy).
It's a tricky intersection on a bit of a slope where motorists often fail to indicate
Cars exiting carpark, don't see cyclists coming from bikepath onto road.
one way road, packed footpaths, no alternative routes to head east

properties.Location: Nancy Bird Way, Brisbane Airport, Queensland
properties.Description: path ends and two lane roundabout is bike and pedestrian hostile

Large radius curves at the intersection mean vehicle entry and exit speeds from Barinia St to Service Rd can be quite high. Particularly vehicles travelling north and down hill along Servcie Rd and turning left into Barinia St. Barinia St is approx 13.1m wide which results in a long crossing time for shared path users.
Hill crest and shoulder disappears.  Fast traffic, no place for bikes to ride.
Road narrows to one lane with no shoulder.  very dangerous for bikes
All of Gailey Rd (from the Sir Fred Schonell intersection to the roundabout) is narrow with vehicles passing very close.

properties.Location: 90 Kelvin Grove Road, Red Hill, Queensland
properties.Description: Extremely narrow footpath next to busy road

properties.Location: 82 Upper Kedron Road, Ferny Grove, Queensland
properties.Description: Bike lane ends, narrow road with island in the middle so cars can't go round. Lots of trucks. Uphill so your going really slow.
Very narrow convergence of stationary and moving bikes and pedestrians on a very busy blind corner
the road with parked cars on both sides is too narrow for the heavy vehicles use this road
This is the link from Western suburbs to Bne River / bikeway. No bike lanes, high level of traffic
Need to go on to road to get around
If you are going inbound on Moggill Rd, it is hard to get out of the left lane before it sends you onto the on-ramp of the Western Freeway
Fast, heavy traffic with large trucks

Between Breakfast Creek and the Valley this road is really unsafe.  It is fast, there are no bike lanes, the drivers are aggressive, don't allow enough space & cut in after passing you.  The surface is bad in patches.
Been a few times when cars poke their bonnets out across the footpath in front of oncoming bikes because the driveways are so narrow they cant see bikes coming down them
The bike lane ends, cyclists presumably disappear
In the morning peak motorists use the bike lane as a left turn lane, despite something like 11 bike lane signs. The traffic frequently banks back to the shooting range. It is also an 80km/h zone.

properties.Location: 50 Lower River Terrace, South Brisbane, Queensland
properties.Description: This is a stop sign at bottom of a hill where cars often going too fast downhill to bother  stopping at stop sign and proceed rapidly into path of cyclists at the busy cycle corssing.
Pavement edge very severe >80mm drop off.
Vehicles park in the no standing zone making it difficult to perform a u- turn when heading from the go between bridge (to then head towards southbank)
Drivers enter the bike lane on the left to turn left. Often will speed past bikes and sharply turn left in front. Must 'take the lane' where possible
100km speed limit and no bike lane
Competition with cars and trucks. See red line on image.

properties.Location: Centenary Highway, Fig Tree Pocket, Queensland
properties.Description:
The speed of some cyclists (you know who you are), is just far too fast. This is not a racetrack.
Especially along this very narrow stretch. But it applies to the entire cycleway.

The state needs legislation to speed limit cyclists for the safety of all casual riders.

There's a footpath being built here but it ends abruptly and is too narrow. Vehicle traffic is too fast to feel safe using the cycle lanes on Compton Rd. There's more than enough space here to put through a fully separated cycleway
There is no shoulder on the crest causing passing cars to squeeze past cyclist on the crest.
vehicles take this turn at high speed. needs vertical deflection to decrease turning speeds.

i always feel nervous riding this section due to the risk of being knocked off my bike by taxi drivers opening their door onto the bike lane.  due to the traffic lights timing, there are often cars or vans adjacent to me, so when a door opens i have nowhere to go.  taxi drivers seem ignorant of the hazard they present

Been car doored twice in this street and taken to hospital in ambulance. Street is often fully parked while lanes are narrow so procession of cars build up behind cyclist with strong pressure to pass cyclist without sufficient room, and strong pressure on cyclist to ride closer to cars.

This section of road between Chalk Street and Wellington Street is extremely dangerous, with people cycling forced to ride in the door zone and/or be close-past by aggressive drivers. It is on the connection between the North Brisbane Bikeway and Kedron Brook westbound via Wellington Street and Lutwyche Busway Station
No bike lane on Brunswick St, footpath is extremely tight with BCC Bus Shelter, Advertising Shells, High Speed & Volume of Car traffic
All of waterworks rd is unsafe for cyclist. Narrow roads, even for cars, high speeds and limited cycling infrastructure along the route.
The footpath is very narrow and the road is very busy.
This is the link from Western Suburbs to Bne River bikeway. Lots of cars and pedestrians, no bike lane, lots of cars trying to park and get to cafes.

properties.Location: 157 Old Northern Road, Albany Creek, Queensland
properties.Description: Between Flamingo Dr and Faheys Rd there is no bike lane or shoulder, the speed is 70, and drivers do not want you in "their" lane. It is a dangerous gap in an otherwise pretty good road.
There is no bike lane and the road narrows just as traffic is accelerating away from the junction.
Poor bike protection for the key linkage from city to valley

The bike lane ends just before, need to look ahead for possible footing due to kerbside parking, but also concerned about commuter cars side-swiping you coming around the corner and up they hill. They want to go around you, but they have to go into the oncoming lane to do so on a blind corner.
riding between parked cars and moving cars is dangerous.
Cyclists have to merge from shoulder of road into two lanes of 70kmph traffic. Reported to BCC five years ago (and chased up regularly without sucess).
Just a little bit of road verge would be ideal for safe uphill on-road cycling with many trucks and cars flying up but no space for slow moving cyclists.
Where is the Toowong Green Bridge that you promised Lord Mayor Schrinner??    It would mean a more connected ride from West End to the west via Sylvan Road and vice versa.
The bike lane just ends heading west, requiring you to cross at a busy intersection to use the too narrow footpath on the other side

Travelling easterly Linkfield road has no bicycle lane between the crossing and the offramp onto the Bruce, resulting in cyclist having to ride in heavy traffic with no protection and having to cross the busy lane, risking your life to get onto a mortally dangerous bridge with zero painted shoulder and trucks pushing for their space.

properties.Location: 79 Camp Mountain Road, Camp Mountain, Queensland
properties.Description: Touted as part of the Samford Cycle Link, this part of the link contains zero infrastructure despite initial planning to add bike lane. I was relativity unworried until I took my kids (and there was a family across the road) and the cars were simply passing too close for comfort.

Terrible footpath prevents ease of access to Witton rd and Ambrose Treacy college.  If the foorpth were remade wider between Bridge st and Twigg st, it could be a useful shared path that would be safer for pedestrians, children on bikes, and commuters
Vehicles frequently parked on this setup of road during the afternoon peak (it is no standing from 3-6pm).

properties.Location: Spring Hill Dog Park, Spring Hill, Queensland
properties.Description: There is no lighting in this section of the bikeway. At night it is pitch black and unsafe. To connect from QUT to the Ekka grounds area is very unsafe.
To travel from the bike lane to Sylvan road you need to make Dangerous left turn across the road.
should take obe side off road for dedicated bike path twoways.
This intersection requires a bike to turn right to get into the stadium precinct to join the Bicentennial bikeway. It is a nightmare.
The road surface is unsealed and it is poorly signed, cars and trucks fail to give way when turning right across Theodore Street to Curtain Ave
To be able to turn right at this intersection, you have to cross 2 lanes of vehicle traffic and then wait for an opening in the 2-lane oncoming traffic
Cars come from many directions at speed and it is difficult to cross
I feel like my life is at risk if I cycle on this road.

properties.Location: 632 Redbank Plains Road, Redbank Plains, Queensland
properties.Description: No bike lane with fast moving cars at peak times
This is the link from western suburbs to Bne River / bikeway. No real bikelanes, lots of traffic and parked cars.
No bike lane, narrow footpath, no alternative crossings nearby

properties.Location: Bowen Hills Railway Station, Bowen Hills, Queensland
properties.Description: It's unsafe to ride from the New Farm peninsula to Bowen Hills train station - it would be great to be able to get to the station by bike!

properties.Location: Fortitude Valley Railway Station, Fortitude Valley, Queensland
properties.Description: It's unsafe to ride from the New Farm peninsula to Fortitude Valley train station - it would be great to be able to get to the station by bike!
This round about is dangerous in a car, let alone a bicycle. The lanes are confusing, the off ramps are narrow and the traffic speeds through here to fit in gaps in traffic when they appear.

cyclingbrisbane.com.au (operated by the council) has this spot listed as a bike lane. I have contacted council about this as there evidently is no space for cycling here. Apparently there's some politics around their Bike Network Overlay which I couldn't really figure out. Cornwall Street is a busy 3 lane, one way road and has cars parked.

Wickham Street from the Valley to Breakfast Creek is a nightmare, particularly during peak hour.  It's too fast, there are no bike lines, drivers are aggressive and impatient, there are unsafe sections where there are fast left exit lanes.
Traffice is usually busy, when trying to get across turning road and into straight on
On the way into work peak hour with no bicycle lane or shoulder to ride on. Cars don't give you enough space
There's no easy and safe way to cross this awful intersection to continue along the bikeway.
Another blind driveway where cars from multiple residences poke bonnet out in front of oncoming bikes on footpath next to very busy road.

Left turning cars merging across the bike lane, often with no regard for cyclists. Have been involved and witnessed multiple instances of cars swerving across in front of cyclists and cars queuing up at the intersection with vehicles backed up and blocking the cycle lane completely. Extremely dangerous and stressful for cyclists here.

properties.Location: Kangaroo Point Cliffs Drive, Kangaroo Point, Queensland
properties.Description: Pedestrains often dont like to follow the path around the tree and often (esp. when running) jump out off footpath onto bike-only lane into path of multiple bikes without looking to take a shortcut. Needs clearer warning signs.
No space at all
Very hard to cross from Moggill Rd to Chapel Hill Rd, and the traffic light sensors don't work for bicycles. I complained to TMR, but they are not replacing the sensors
Bike lane comes to an end with traffic turning in different directions.  Avoid the area too dangerous.
Combination of heavy traffic at most times of day, but primarily narrow lanes with negligible shoulder make the experience hair-raising.

properties.Location: 708 Old Cleveland Road, Camp Hill, Queensland
properties.Description:
From starting at the Caltex service station at Bennett rd all the way to Carina Old Cleveland Road is very dangerous 
Buses and cars travel too close. There is risk of being car doored at the camphill school. The road is eneven surface after Camphill from Red rooster to Creek Road Carina

The southbound lane of Nudgee Rd., at this point, pinches down to a single lane with no shoulder. It is a location that is very busy and requires confidence to navigate due to up close vehicle interactions.

The road is a main road that connects suburbs.  Between the railway and the highway it is dangerous for cyclists as the speed limit is 80.  Gravel trucks use the road and there is no shoulder.  I detour around this road, adding several km to my ride.
Sometimes cars just won't let you in  a lane to cross
Wynnum Rd needs a regular sweeper to drive along the bikeway and shoulders to clean-up the "marbles" which include glass and loose gravel. Very unsafe to ride on and end up riding on road with cars.

A small ramp and path needs to connect to Railway Street heading northeast. This has been "promised" by the local cr but no timeframe has been given. I have witnessed many small falls and close shaves by cyclists through the lack of this facility.

1. If you use the bike button to get the green lights they don't stay green long enough for more than 1-2 bikes to get through.
2. Cars turning left onto Jephson St with bikes going straight to milton rd.  Very confusing and stressful for bikes and cars.   Accident waiting to happen here.
Too much at peak times

This crossing is busy so bikes can get in each others way.  Heading outbound is an uphill take off, cars come very quickly off the roundabout up miskin st and it's hard to see which cars are going to make the turn.  
Very tricky spot for riders that aren't confident.
Road narrows no room for traffic to overtake easily
Road is pinched by parked cars forcing you into the lane on a blind corner with narrow lanes

For most of Blunder Rd along this stretch on the west side there is at least some chance of unprotected riding space within the car parking. Ideally, it should be protected.
On the east side there is zero infrastructure.
Cars overtaking in a dangerous manner on bridge 5and abusing me for being there
Not that I'd recommend that anyone actually ride on Moggill Rd but the painted lane just ends and there isn't even a ramp up to the footpath.

This roundabout is one of the worst intersections in the area. A typical roundabout for Brisbane with painted bike lanes on the entry and exit of the roundabout that place riders in a dangerous position. All of the bike lanes in this intersection are worn from vehicle traffic. All of them are driven in by drivers and no space is given for riders.
Narrow bridge, aggressive drivers the entire length of Sherwood Rd
Very difficult to cross Ekibin Road East at any location as a cyclist or pedestrian. High vehicles volumes and speeds and no facilities.
It's a designated bike lane but BCC allow parking making it unsafe to use.  You have to ride outside the bike lane to be outside the door zone
very busy road where the cycle lane just ends at the bridge, forcing riders onto the main carriageway.  At peak times, the traffic is constant with 80km/h speed limit.

Bike lane ends abruptly on a hill with the road narrowing in a 70km/h zone and (effectively) only one straight ahead car lane (centre lane is turn or straight but is always occupied by right turning vehicles). A treacherous spot for cyclists in peak times.
Heading south from the city, this is a tricky spot to negotiate.  I go straight ahead from the shared path to the road at a signalised intersection where many cars are turning left.
Unsafe intersection for people riding and walking. No space. Poor driver behaviour and always debris on road. Fast speeds and requires crossing left-turn lanes. High stress as bike lanes stop/start.
The left turning traffic signal goes before the straight ahead traffic  which is dangerous for cyclists proceeding straight ahead.
ind corner with over grown veg

properties.Location: 119 Pacific Motorway, Holland Park West, Queensland
properties.Description: way too narrow, design out dated as so much more cyclist traffic now need widening
Path too old outdated, too many people using it, need widening ASAP
This spot is ver dangerous, not enough space on the footpath for riders pedestrians, traffic signs etc.
Extremely high chance of getting hit or knocked off bicycle due to narrow pinch point

This link to Price Street has door zones in both directions, with heavy traffic, particularly at peak times. Drivers are aggressive and frequently pass closely. This is on Brisbane City Council's recommended connection for people cycling from the North Brisbane Bikeway to Chermside. The intersection with Rose Street also has a dangerous slip lane.
No shoulder at all, narrow road, lots of traffic as this is a main connector road,  80km/hr zone.   Local cyclists avoid this section of road.  Nicknamed "suicide alley"
The footpath isn't safe and the road is too busy, with cars veering into the far left to turn onto Riding Road.
No shoulder on the road and you have to ride up between a turnign lane and go straight lane with no dedicated bike lane
no footpath on bike path on both side of road, it does not connect slacls creek green link to compton road V1, very vety dangerous
Require a minimum of a wide shared path to connect Nissan Arena (Major event attraction) with V1 off Laver St. Should promote event goers to ride bikes instead of drive

Put a V1 along Captain Cook Bridge. People ride into city to work commute, they do not want to get off lower river Tce  ride via Go Between Bridge cause it is in direct. iat least 50% of the active transport will be reduced at the ship inn intersection, alot less chances for collision / accidents.
Very busy road with lots of trucks and a poor riding surface.
Cars misjudge the width and pass too close. Also trucks enter the bike lane that starts with their trailers
Crossing over to continue on redland bay road towards capalaba is very nerve recking traveling at 50km/h. Drivers do not follow the safe distance.

Its very rare to see a car stop at the stop sign because its a very steep hill and they don't like to do hill starts so I would say 90% dive through it - often into path of oncoming bikes. Needs to be an extra sign or extra policing here or a traffic behaviour monitring befther someone is hurt.
very dangerous corner turn over bike path

properties.Location: 417 Cleveland Redland Bay Road, Victoria Point, Queensland
properties.Description: There is no connection  between path systems on east and west of the Cleveland -Redland Bay road. this could simply be fixed with an underpass on the northern most bridge of Eprapah Creek. This would also assist all school kids and residential in getting to the shopping precinct safely.

High speed traffic with no safe cycling infrastructure. Moggill Rd from Indooroopilly to Toowong really needs a dedicated separated cycleway. I've seen many close passes of courier riders who use this road to deliver food from Toowong Village and Indooroopilly Shopping Centre.

Large truck pass close to people in the bike lane because the bike lane is too narrow.  The bike lane used to be wider but council narrowed it to make a turning lane for the new shopping centre.  This was poor planning by the council  who should have made the shopping centre give up some land for the turn lane into the shopping centre.
No signage on a busy intersection.

As you ride up the hill there is a lot of traffic where the road is narrow in both directions. As you reach the top of the hill there us a traffic island that forces the traffic even closer to any cyclists.
No room on shoulder, blind rollers , cars at speed

On road bike lane ends uphill in a 70km/hr traffic zone. There is a path on the footpath but the entry is blocked by a tree, and there is no ramp into the kerb so the only option is to merge into traffic doing 70km/hr. And because it's in Logan the drivers will then try to kill you. I couldn't design a more dangerous spot if i tried.
There is a bike lane, but it ends at the back of the cars parked here. You need to be confident and take the lane to use this road.
Large amounts of debris on road shoulder, no street lighting and high speed traffic creates crash hazard
The Centenary Cycleway was intended to continue on to Springfield, but was cancelled in 2012. It is now extremely unsafe to ride to Springfield from here. Needs to be built.

Heading north on Ipswich Road before Stanley St, there is a painted bike lane (that ends on Stanley St) and 3 lanes of through traffic. After you cross Stanley street the left lane ends & has to merge into the centre lane. The painted bike lane at this intersection creates a false sense of safety.

Turning lane into Montrose/Moorak crosses cycle lane and creates major conflict point. Leaves cyclists in dangerous road position on high speed road.  Skid marks in cycle lane show how dangerous this section is.

properties.Location: 63 Klumpp Road, Upper Mount Gravatt, Queensland
properties.Description: No bike lane, no shoulder, 60km/h busy car traffic, lots of lane changing after the motorway offramp intersection, cars turning right block traffic causing vehicles behind to change lanes, very poor condition of footpaths.
Bus waiting point and bus stop blocks bike lane through to intersection. Traffic volumes and hostile drivers make it difficult to overtake buses waiting for passengers.
Cars queuing to turn onto highway move left to allow others vehicles to pass to their right. The cars on the left obscure the green painted bike lane
Narrow, rough and high speed traffic

Very high frequency use, and the path kicks you out onto the wrong side of the road needing to get across. Often a lot of traffic. Difficult manoeuvre and whilst looking behind at traffic over shoulder you risk running into your buddy, or hitting a car head on. Poorly linked up path on a major bikeway

After an uninterrupted glide down from Mt Cootha on paths, or from along the freeway paths you are rudely spat out onto a road where you genuinely shut your eyes and hope. It is blindish corner with potential buses coming in from the right. Not good design, albeit rider behaviour. But very high frequency path - silly design.
Narrow painted bike lane putting cyclists too close to cars near a busy intersection and a known road avoided by many cyclists

properties.Location: 2 Savanna Place, Eight Mile Plains, Queensland
properties.Description: blind corner, bad design

No safe way to get to/from station (on a bike) from new estates. Non existant bike lanes even though the road is very wide, heavy vehicles, fast cars exiting Gympie Rd. Station is so close and ride is mostly flat, but so hard to get to.
dangerous crossing for ped n cyclist
There are also no traffic lights. The roadway is narrow, very poor surface. The cars are at 90kmh. To turn right into woogaroo st is a nightmare.

This cycle lane on the right hand side is not convenient or practice to access for any cyclist coming from Stanley St or Dock St bikeways and heading towards Grey St. They're going to use the footpath on the park side of Vulture St instead.
Very narrow over bridge. No shoulder on the road from the bridge to the intersection.
Too dark in the evening or at night, NO LIGHTS!!??? high speed coming down hill on narrow out dated bike path design. Lack of shades, open to M1 vehicular traffic with noise and fume
old path too narrow lots more people using it needs widening and upgrading
Path too old outdated, too many people using it, need widening ASAP
Lack of space and high road speed limit with heavy vehicles
old narrow 0.9m single sided footpath, horror wjth no alternative

properties.Location: 495 Old Cleveland Road, Camp Hill, Queensland
properties.Description: Road narrows significantly on a downhill corner where vehicles are gaining speed. This is the most dangerous part of my commute each day.
Pedestrians often walking on bike-only track without paying attention to warning bells. Often with airpods, dogs etc. Needs more warnings on bike path that its a bike only path.
We need a safe way to get into our suburb from the Bikeway
No bike or even footpath along Wacol Station Rd means cyclists and pedestrians are forced to ride or walk along the side of the road. Poor lighting and kangaroos in the area add to the danger.

properties.Location: 1259 Mount Crosby Road, Mount Crosby, Queensland
properties.Description: It is a very steep uphill so cycling speed is very slow, there is no space for traffic to pass and it is a blind crest. Some trucks can't afford to slow down to cycling speed because they wouldn't get up the hill.
There should be a dedicated bike path here along park side to connect D.M. Henderson Park with Kessels Road
An informal path with high volume of usage, should formalise it
Busy rd no bike lane section
No visibility around the corner..
Unsafe cycle diversion due to Buranda station upgrade

A very wide and generally quiet road with no marked shoulder or bicycle lane. And yet every single time I have ridden it I have been aggressively close passed and then abused by a motorist. There's literally metres of room to mark a shoulder or bicycle lane on here. Do it.

properties.Location: 253 Kelvin Grove Road, Kelvin Grove, Queensland
properties.Description: This is a very steep hill. There is no provision for bicycles. This is a main thoroughfare for motor vehicles. Cara caught behind slow cyclists (due to the steep hill) causes a lot of angst. Seek an alternate route.
Too many commercial vehicles pulling on and off the street, no bike lanes, doors opening onto the road.
Many cars parked here that could be a bike lane.
You come from a "End of bike lane" and you're in the traffic. This happens everywhere in Collingwood Park.

properties.Location: 2116 Mount Samson Road, Samford Valley, Queensland
properties.Description: No bike lanes and speed limit 100kms/ hr

properties.Location: 74 Kelvin Grove Road, Red Hill, Queensland
properties.Description: High traffic volume, difficult to change lanes, no bike lane, narrow pavement as alternative
Cycle lane missing on this side of the road for about 100m before the shoulder opens up again a bit further up, high vehicle speed makes it completely unsafe to ride through this part.

Shoulder southbound completely disappears before the crest of a hill. And despite there being a whole empty lane to drive in, truck drivers like to narrow pass within millimetres here. An absolute death zone for cyclists.

In the south direction from this spot the riding lanes are ok, as they are wide, and dispite being on a high speed road.
In the north direction there are no riding lanes at all. The lanes on both sides of the road cease to exist at this point.

Enogerra Creek Bikeway runs parallel with Waterworks Road, a busy 4 lane road. The exit from Ashgrove Golf Club crosses the Bikeway. There are Stop signs on the exits but drivers looking for a gap in the road traffic ignore these and pull out across the Bikeway without looking for bicycles.  Drivers also turn in off Waterworks road without looking.

Highly used area by pedestrians, scooters and cyclists. This short section between Chasley St and the cycleway underpass is very dangerous for all users. Walking ones bike only causes more obstruction and possible injury.
Too difficult to cross.

No safe crossing area from Bayliss Rd path to Wayne Goss Dr and into Berrinba Wetlands path. Riders forced to enter dual lane roundabout with heavy traffic from both directions along Browns Plains road. Drivers often blinded alongside large heavy trucks servicing these commercial areas.

This is a passageway that everyone uses but there is barely room for two cars let alone all the trucks and bikes and pedestrians. There is also no fence between the narrow footpath and the narrow road. This road is terrifying. Old

Northbound there is no shoulder, no separation from traffic including lots of heavy vehicles. Not even a white line. NO GO ZONE. Southbound is ok for the brave outside of peak, but a long left turn lane into Boundary is problematic at all hours, not recommended.

properties.Location: 1031 Logan Road, Holland Park West, Queensland
properties.Description: Cars turning left into Marshall Road, cars parked in the left lane. This is a busy 60km/h road.
Traveling along Moggill Rd towards city, drivers will often cut in front of riders path into the turning lane.

They just ripped up part of the road and built a brand-new intersection, but failed to put a footpath and pedestrian crossing on the opposite side of the road to a major shopping centre. They also made no cycling infrastructure despite having space for a ginormous median island.

This is the natural spot for all the children who live Southwest of this spot to cross Payne Road in order to go to any of the local schools or to get onto the Enoggera Creek Bikeway. There is no crossing and the visibility to cross is poor. Payne Road is busy 8-8:40am and 2:50-3:30pm.

Arnwood Pl is the main access route to the arterial V1 bikeway and local childcare centre but there is no safe way to turn right in/out at Ekibin Rd East / Arnwood Pl. The turnoff is on a sharp bend in the road, and drivers speed round without watching for cyclists. I've been aggressively beeped here while riding with kids on the bike many times.
There is no direct connection to QUT.

There is so much traffic through this area. People frequently get Ubers to this area and when traffic is stop, open their doors. I was doored here recently. The lane is way too narrow - it's one of the worst spots is Brisbane.
Car did not give way, drove straight and side swiped bicycle. Bicycle rider was completely taken out and landed heavily on road.
Scary to navigate depending on traffic volumes

properties.Location: 21 Jack Flynn Memorial Drive, Morningside, Queensland
properties.Description: poor lighting, lots of delayed uncontrolled traffic movements - as a cyclist you are lost and a very low priority - happy to get through alive everytime

properties.Location: 96 Messines Ridge Road, Holland Park West, Queensland
properties.Description:
This whole stretch is a mess. If I try to take Messines Ridge, which by the bike lane's placement it indicates I ought to (even though it's not safe as that bike lane ends almost immediately), then I have to cross Gaza Road, but cars are really flying through here.
Trying to go under the M3 to access the V1 also feels very dangerous.

Actively avoid this spot, so unsafe. Intersection is designed for cars, so people on bikes and foot have to use unprotected refuge islands, or if on road, are sandwiched in-between turn and through lanes, and then maintain speed on uphill, with business driveways and merge lanes (high cognitive load).
The on road bike lane gets taken over by vehicles, particularly BCC buses, that are trying to avoid the median. Vehicles need to slow down and wait for the bicycles to have cleared the bottleneck.
Cars drive too close and at speed
Path too old outdated, too many people using it, need widening ASAP
Horror able narrow bridge

properties.Location: Inner City Bypass, Red Hill, Queensland
properties.Description: Narrow spot with bad visibility by nature of the path course geometry. Bus stop positioned at worst possible location, bus shelter making things even more narrow and obstructed
Heavy vehicle traffic and almost unusable width bike lane
Narrow road,  heavy trucks use this road
Too narrow and old bridge, 0.9m wide, can only fit 1 person through at a time, need upgrade / widening ASAP
Only enough room for a person to navigate.
Path too old outdated, too many people using it, need widening ASAP
No shoulder or bike lane and cars doing 70kmh trying to push you off the road
Coming out the city and riding up Kemp Pl you are unable to get onto the footpath of the story bridge due to kerbing, so you get caught in 3 lanes of traffic in peak hour

properties.Location: 42 Old Northern Road, Everton Park, Queensland
properties.Description: Awful busy road with no bike infrastructure whatsoever. Hill makes you slow on traffic. There is a sign which has a bike signal on it which is effectively useless
narrow 0 9m old long single side path on only one side of the road

properties.Location: 53 Cressbrook Street, Eight Mile Plains, Queensland
properties.Description: There should be a path along here as all users been informally using it, this is most direct, check strava heat map

properties.Location: 42 Gaza Road, Holland Park West, Queensland
properties.Description: There should be bike paths both side to connect V1 exit from Gaza road exit

this is most direct connection for V1 to the West  (Moorooka & Yeronga), many many cyclist use this, however extremely dangerous with down and up hill speedy traffic and high volume, need at least get ride of on street carparks 1 side and give it a safe lane for cyclist

The traffic speeds over the hill and is moving at 60-80Km past a very narrow footpath with no protection from the passing traffic. Pedestrians, school kids, cyclists do not have the room to pass. Very dangerous and a risk to someone being hit
No dedicated shoulder for cyclists. Many cars and trucks are parked here, leaving very little room for cyclists on this busy road.
No bike lanes, terrible footpath, busy industrial road
No safe cycing routes through this part of the city
This is just a horrible busy junction designed solely for cars with zero consideration of bicyclists. No verge, no bike lanes, merges after the intersection. Dangerous!
4 lanes of traffic travelling at high speeds and no lanes for bikes
Pedestrians, have popped out of stair well, in front of oncoming cyclists many times.
large intersection with no bike lanes
formalise thos path as many ppl already usi ng it especially as a crossing
Scary gap which should be made appropriate for bike commuters and recreational cyclists of all levels to link the inner city to the northern bikeways
Narrow edge of road, high volume and speed of traffic.
No traffic light on busy intersection, with converging traffic from multiple directions, very difficult to cross on foot or bicycle safety in time
There is nowhere to ride your bike to Prince Charles Hospital if you are coming from Kedron Brook Bikeway. The cars drivers are often distracted, running late, stopping for coffee or distracted.
Poor visibility left curve for cars going towards centenary highway, no bicycle lane, no , shoulder, no footpath.

properties.Location: 73 Mount Gravatt Outlook Drive, Mount Gravatt, Queensland
properties.Description: Cars will not wait, they pull out to pass cyclists even thought cyclists are coming from the other direction.
Bike lane heading East along Green Road stops. Crossing is not safe. No path down Waller Road, or continuing on Green road without being forced onto arrow road.
Potential to be knocked off bicycle by cars turning left through short cycle lane into car parks without indicting or indicating too late.

Traffic turning left are not patient for you to cross into the right hand turning lane, traffic in the right hand turning lane have difficulty seeing you cross over if there are cars behind you in the left hand lane.
Cars travel at 80kmh and there are unusable/poor surface shoulders along the entire section of road. Driver behaviour is hit and miss and terrible from some.
This is a very narrow section that joins Samford to the city

properties.Location: 522-526 Beenleigh - Redland Bay Road, Carbrook, Queensland
properties.Description: Not enough room along this whole road to Redland bay with trucks it's a real risk factor
Cars & trucks not giving enough space

properties.Location: 372 Stapylton - Jacobs Well Road, Stapylton, Queensland
properties.Description: Trucks driving within 1 metre at 100km/hr

Re-entering onto the road from the bike path is tricky, requiring a rubber neck and some good luck to avoid a car on Twenty-Fifth or coming along the Hornibrook Hwy. 
You tend to be moving fast - and often in a group as ALL bunch riders are funnelled onto the bridge path. Getting back onto the road a potentially dangerous affair.

There is a level crossing at this location at an angle to the direction of travel. Following rain or humid weather the train tracks become slippery and can cause tyres to lose grip. Further, there is no separate cycle lane and should you lose control of your bike and fall this can easily be into the trafficked lane.

properties.Location: 152a Mount Coot-Tha Road, Mount Coot-Tha, Queensland
properties.Description: Big problem of getting tired riders coasting at 59.9km/hr down Cootha over across traffic and back onto path. It's messy. Cars in their defence have a sweeping corner from the Freeway and aren't expecting a 10 strong bunch of MAMILs trying to work out which on-ramp/off-ramp to hit to get up onto path.
The majority of road between Brisbane to Logan is classified as cycle track yet has no cycle lane ( or footpath) the two councils should get together & try to fix it.
Major gap in the bicycle network. No shoulder and poor surface on the edges of the road. Long and straight so despite 40km/h speed limit cars go fast down the road.
There is zero infrastructure here for active transport. Cars travel here at 80-90km/h in a 70km/h. Currently the only safe way is to make a 15 minute detour through North Lakes.

Southbound there is no bicycle infrastructure and a left hand traffic lane that ends at the top of the hill. Every time I have tried to ride up this I am aggressively passed by a motorist somehow unable to navigate themselves into the right hand lane despite knowing their lane ends in a few metres anyway. Traffic or no traffic.

Crossing and riding along Nudgee Road very scary. 80km traffic. Trucks. Poor surface. The "bike path" is NOT a bike path for a cycling bunch. It is for a family ride. **No families ride out past the dump.
Redcliffe's East-West Roads like MacDonnell Road generally have very wide bike lanes but still feel unsafe and unusable due to the speed of car traffic. Needs physical separation.

Major road with high speed traffic and bicycle lane just ends with no exit to footpath or other safe exit. Leaves riders on a high speed road, up hill section, on a narrowing lane. Additionally there is no signage that the lane is ending.

properties.Location: Springfield Greenbank Arterial, Springfield Lakes, Queensland
