properties.Description: The shoulder heading southeast from here is about a foot wide (at most). There are no kerb ramps to get off the road so you have to enter the footpath from the Springfield Lakes Blvd pedestrian crossing
The bike lanes end abruptly on a 60km/h road 3 lanes per direction of travel

The pedestrian lights are almost always red. Would be good to have it always green except when drivers from Oakey Flat Road enter it or if people turning left from Morayfield Rd trigger the left turn.

Footpath is poor for cycling
 The road is busy, 70km/h, has lots of trucks, no bike lane and no shoulder.
Another blind corner that impatient drivers attempt to overtake riders forced into the lane by parked cars.

Major connecting route between Indooroopilly and Toowong and there is only very disconnected small sections of on-road bicycle lanes. Highly dangerous route. Riding on the footpath is also not a decent option as it is broken an involves many dangerous road crossings.
Quality multi-use paths up to this intersection but there is a large gap through a high speed dangerous intersection. Often used by children and requires them to use the road.
Street everyone uses to cut through during peak traffic to avoid busy intersections.

Very unsafe corner, even at slow speeds. Combined bicycle and pedestrian two-way traffic, completely blind corner, immediately adjacent major road intersection with no barrier at all. There is plenty of spare road width to widen footpath to make two lanes, add an automatic flashing light for oncoming bikes, and separate from the road with barrier.

This road is the only walk/ride to/from school/train for many local residents.

It is a HEAVILY used rat run and is very steep. 20% near the top.

The surface is rocky and the road narrow yet cars speed at 60km/h even cms away from pedestrians.

Riding up that hill in school hours or around peak commute is terrifying. Council ignores requests.

There is no reason the whole length of Swann Rd here doesn't have cycle lanes. The road is very wide and has enough vacant car parks that removing one side of parking for safe cycle lanes would be an easy addition without affecting residents.

properties.Location: 4 Gaza Road, Holland Park West, Queensland
properties.Description: lots of traffic , no bike lanes, footpath extremely narrow with handrails , un desirable for anyone to ride through, including school kids from near Holland Park West State High, so ended up more parents driving their kids to school even they live 5mins ride from home

Two reasons; when crossing the road as a cyclist or on foot, the turning traffic can start turning as the light flashes red. There isn't enough time to get across so cars start turning infront of you or creeping up to you and its very dangerous. Cars should just wait and not have the option to turn just like all other traffic signals.

Unsafe to use for 2 months every year due to aggressive magpie. Forced to go via Sir Fred Schonell Drive instead. Better a low chance of severe injury than a 100% chance of minor assault, but it's not a judgment we should be forced to make.
Colleges Crossing is the only link into Karalee and Ipswich. Mt Crosby Road doesn't have infrastructure, and is steep, so very dangerous.
There's no bike lane and cars turn left into Ridge Street from around the corner. Lots of traffic during peak hour. I nearly got hit here a while back.

properties.Location: 1012 Waterford - Tamborine Road, Logan Village, Queensland
properties.Description: Road narrows with no space for cyclists through cutting and over Quinzeh Creek Bridge on this busy 80kph Main Road
Up to that point, there is road shoulder but it stops before the roundabout and there's no protection on the roundabout.
Bike lane suddenly ends on a busy road. Then, nothing reappears until the intersection between Stanley and Main Streets, past the Gabba!
Bike lane northbound simply ends, at the same time the shoulder ends. Passing car traffic at 80km/hr with about 2cm of shoulder to ride on. Absolutely no idea how this is thought to be acceptable.

Bikeway to the airport starts and stops haphazardly. Wayfinding signs at junctions on bikeway are either non-existent or pointing in wrong directions. Bikeway leads you to the wrong way on a one-way street at some points. Bikeway turns into a painted gutter on what is essentially a highway.
Cycle way suddenly ends right outside a school. Not suitable for school students to safely commute to school
Dangerous and narrow entry to path and bridge
Dangerous corner, blind corner with 100% not transparent fence blocking fast sharp corner
Bikes going West to East on Grevilla Rd have the right of way, but cars going South to North fail to stop at the stop sign and give way to bikes. I have several near misses.
unsafe intersection

There is no dedicated bicycle path to continue into Parton Street in a Southerly direction. Travelling Easterly direction, turning Southerly into Parton one has to either risk traffic, or use a very slow and cumbersome traffic call button on the traffic light which only exists on the western side of the intersection. Nothing on the eastern side.
The road should be one way with a bike lane that extends through the park from the carpark
Multiple people enter this intersection and the plants and infrastructure make it a blind intersection and very dangerous

Trouts road has a generally wide shoulder (which unfortunately has many cars that result in car dooring risk), but at this particular spot the wide shoulder disappears into a very narrow shoulder around a corner.
How
There is no road shoulder and its steep in both directions - leading to slow bicycle speed and poor driver behaviour.
The bicycle lane is too narrow, has cars parked in it. Whenever I'm cycling up, I'm always in precarious positions.
This used to be the safe route from Ipswich until traffic islands were put in & narrowed the path. Very poor road design I would have expected better from a project only a few years old.

This footpath is an important part of the north-south active transport route from Ashgrove to Enoggera without having to do major detours, however it feels incredibly unsafe using it. It is narrow, overgrown, cracked, and very close to 60km/h traffic.
Narrow lanes and bad driving (speed) using this road as a rat run

The road narrows quite considerably as the bridge is narrow.  The shoulder gets cut off.  And impatient drivers don't allow a bicycle to merge into the traffic.  I no longer go this route if possible.

Chiswick Rd is part of the connection between Ithaca Cr Bikeway and Mt Cootha. Bikeway disappears and this is a common link - high level of traffic >50km/hr zone never enforced, lots of traffic entering from Cecil Rd and Bowman Pde into busy Simpsons and Chiswick Rd intersection. Lots of school, park and kindy kids mixed with traffic.
missing a direct crossing from voygayor drv to compton road, need that urgently
narrow 0 9m old long single side path on only one side of the road
narrow 0 9m old long single side path on only one side of the road
narrow 0 9m old long single side path on only one side of the road
narrow 0 9m old long single side path on only one side of the road
Its shown on PCN as a link, this will be great to have away from Beenleigh road for connection

properties.Location: 98 Esher Street, Holland Park West, Queensland
properties.Description: bridge is old, not wide enough path for both pedestrian and cyclist, it is an important crossing for all users to cross over M1
High volume usage of informal path, should just formalise it,

properties.Location: 720 Redbank Plains Road, Redbank Plains, Queensland
properties.Description: This the only access to Ipswich from Redbank Plains unless you go 10km further to go around
Parked cars on downhill stretch mean I often move out a little from the far left and consequently drivers often perform very dangerous (fast) overtaking maneuvers here.
Tight corner on road with parked cars, drivers often perform dangerous overtaking maneuvers around corner.
Drivers can be very aggressive and there is no bike markings at all so people think you should be on the footpath. It is also 1km to the Ennoggera Creek Bikeway
No room for bicycles along this road, and no safe options for school children wanting to cycle to school
No bicycle lane
4 lanes of high speed traffic and no lanes for bikes. The intersection is also unsignalised and drivers do not give way to bike riders or pedestrians when turning
There are no bike lanes and I never ride here because it's a busy road.  I don't have a car and only my bike as a form of transport.   Woolworths and my doctor are down here
I do not feel safe riding on the road and I would certainly not feel safe for my children to ride on the road either.
lots of cars, parked cars, buses, uphill meaning travelling slower on the bike. Would like to ride to shops but its too hard with no safe bike lane.
Beaudesert Rd is a major arterial road with no bicycle facilities and no parallel "back streets" route. There is no direct route heading into the city from here.
No bicycle facilities on Ipswich Rd, which is the most direct route from Salisbury, Moorooka and Annerley towards South Brisbane and the City.

To reach the Elisabeth St bikepath, I must exit Gotha St, cross the 4 lanes of traffic here to the second-from-left lane, while cars on either side seek the leftmost lane but also avoid parked buses in that lane. At peak hour it is very busy and fast with people concentrating on getting to their turning lane.

Ah, Kedron. They really know how to funnel traffic into tight spaces in these parts. Wayland St can be *terrifying* as each and every roaring SUV hurtles past in a desperate bid to reach traffic lights (somewhere) 1st. Connectivity for non-vehicle users is abysmal in this suburb. Other suburbs establish laneways and other treatments, not Kedron.

bike lane ends just before round about and starts again after round about, this is a large round about with poor visibility, this is a dual lane round about in a 70kph zone and traffic passes cyclists very close and fast.

I've been knocked down twice by drivers turning left into the service station at this corner into the service station.  On both occasions the driver didn't see me, came from behind me and turned into me as I was heading east on Telegraph Road.  3 broken ribs on one occasion.  I ride past here 6 days a week and am always wary at this point.

Too dangerous - bike lane is merged between vehicle lanes, slight uphill to compete with slowing people on bikes down with fast cars and always lots of cars. Only time to ride on this is first thing in the morning, otherwise, forget it.
Cars use the bike lane to turn left so if I am in the bike lane, cars turning left will turn into my path. Have to enter the car lane to avoid being run over.
Hazardous right turn into Nathan St from rear approaching traffic as there is no turning lane.

properties.Location: 11 Stapylton - Jacobs Well Road, Stapylton, Queensland
properties.Description: The road is too narrow for a major road with lots of heavy vehicles
The installed median strip in the middle of the road narrows the lane so that a bike and car can't proceed through safely together.
Dangerous conditions if you need to move across lanes
Very narrow rough edge on a busy road with no alternative route
Road condition is terrible (including large potholes and bumps) and unsafe for the cyclists that regularly use the street
Narrow, busy road. No bike lanes. Lots of traffic, including numerous lanes getting to left for ICB

Bike lane on road "ends l and expects groups to exit the road and up on a path. This simply doesn't happen due to dangerous and impractical little path. Often you cycle in a group of 4-20 here, and have just exited a wide marked path @ about 45km/hr. No one exits up onto a silly path for 30m. Instead you take the lane, and cop abuse.
Merging traffic from airport with round-about (where bikes are coming from). Distracted drivers, difficult merge to get to the left.
Large round-a-bout with fast traffic, little infrastructure. Cars in hurry to get to int airport.

Riding a mountain bike along Dillon Rd between Bonros Place and Highwood Rd is a very dangerous endeavour because there is no bike lane, the road is narrow and windy and drivers exceed the speed limit. I risk my life on the road to go ride off-road!

No bike lane, 3 lanes into 2 and on a corner, into a slight rise.
Lots of busy traffic. Dangerous.

properties.Location: Gap Creek Road, Mount Coot-Tha, Queensland
properties.Description: Council, in their wisdom, has installed traffic 'calming' islands with no bike bypass on this very steep, heavily trafficked hill. This is awful as a cyclist when you're climbing a 7-8% gradient at 15km/hr and the shoulder repeatedly closes in front of you as cars drive past doing 60km/hr+. Terrible road design.

The curb build out forces cyclists into the single lane with motorists.  Curbs on both sides of the road mean cars can't safely pass bikes, forcing drivers to slow down to bike speed, which they hate and unsafe behaviour ensues.
Terrible surface. Lots of traffic. Too narrow.
Small hill gather speed. Terrible surface. Lots of cars.
Redcliffe's East-West Roads like MacDonnell Road generally have very wide bike lanes but still feel unsafe and unusable due to the speed of car traffic. Needs physical separation.
People in cars aren't looking for people on the footpath.
No way for people on bikes to cycle into shopping centre easily to park.

This corner is quite narrow and the "kerb marking" effectively disappears meaning you have to take the corner in the lane. Cars attempt to overtake on what is a blind corner. Council plan to solve the problem by putting a concrete divider (!!!) in the middle to narrow it even further.

properties.Location: 100 Spring Mountain Drive, New Beith, Queensland
properties.Description: Narrow bridges with no shoulder, trucks and traffic 80 to 100 kph.
Uphill section on narrow road with fast speeds. Drivers are impatient and perform dangerous passes.
This shopping centre has no access for bikes. It also has no bike racks.
The traffic lights don't allow for bike riders to cross separately from cars. Would be better if the intersection did all pedestrian crossings for a longer period and more frequently.
The infrastructure for pedestrians on Morayfield Rd is only workable on 1 side.
It's horrible for anyone not in a car.
No ramps to ride on the footpaths.
No ramps to get on the footpath
Brunswick street is a busy road with no lanes for bikes. It's the main route between New Farm and the Valley
It's an intimidating design with square edges that make it difficult to cross easily.
Some of these areas are hidden behind fences so when you're cycling along the footpath, drivers can't see if you're coming.

Very unsafe corner, even at slow speeds. Combined bicycle and pedestrian two-way traffic, completely blind corner, immediately adjacent major road intersection with no barrier at all. There is plenty of spare road width to widen footpath to make two lanes, add an automatic flashing light for oncoming bikes, and separate from the road with barrier.
Getting across Samford Road is hard. The signalised pedestrian crossing is narrow and chicaned by fencing, the traffic lanes are busy with poor visibility.
Narrow lane around corner and on uphill section results in impatient drivers attempting to squeeze cyclists into parked cars. Cyclists need to take the lane here or are in danger.

In front of school, drivers dropping children at school produce major conflict points with cyclists passing through or riding to school. Road narrows around corner and cars stop illegally in no stopping zones increase danger.

Major connecting route, with wide road, but parked cars force driver conflicts. Impatient aggressive drivers perform fast passes on dangerous sections of road, particularly around blind corners. Needs a separated cycle path.

The footpath outside the restaurants doubles up as off-street car parking, so drivers don't think to give way to pedestrians. Two recent developments have made the driveway crossovers even more dangerous for pedestrians and riders rather than safer.
This is on the main cycle corridor into Brisbane city from the South West. The excellent cycling infrastructure ends here and requires cyclists to cross a heavy-use road adjacent to a bus terminal.

Sylvan Road is the main connector between two major bikeways, carries over 3000 cyclists per day, but has nothing but painted bike lanes, no parking times that motorists ignore, and dangerous intersections. Needs protected bike lanes.
No road shoulder and a lot of heavy vehicle traffic. The road is used by many cyclists going to Nudgee Beach or Boondall wetlands.
The footpath is also dangerously narrow and steep

No cycling infrastructure requiring bike users to ride on a high speed road or in the shoulder where trucks are often parked. Unsafe location to be doored or run over by a parked vehicle reentering the roadway.

Green paint only does not stop car drivers cutting into the 'bike lane' on this 'curve to the left' piece of road.
Brisbane City Council has resisted putting a protected dedicated bike lane here for many years. This road links two of Brisbane's biggest pieces of cycle infrastructure and doesn't even service very many cars.

This is a busy intersection with cyclists and pedestrians heading towards the CBD, Qld Uni and BSSSC, coming from three different directions, funneled across an intersection with two different slip-lanes onto a narrow footpath with poor visibility.  Local residents have been asking for improvements but nothing so far.
Cars regularly ignore the right of way for bikes and drive through bikeway without looking. Be careful cycling through this intersection

Fagan Rd & Aberleigh Rd is part of major cycling route from the Northside to the City.  Crossing Butterfield St is difficult and dangerous as it is uphill (so cyclists are moving slowly) and Butterfield St is a very wide street to cross, so traffic moves fast.  It is difficult for cyclists to see traffic approaching from either direction.

This is the primary route from the Bicentennial Bikeway to the University of Queensland, and the path along Brisbane Street is just shockingly narrow and inadequate. 4 lanes for motor vehicles, one could easily be repurposed as a cycleway.
Cars regularly ignore the right of way for bikes and drive through bikeway without looking. Be careful cycling through this intersection

properties.Location: 27 Holmead Road, Eight Mile Plains, Queensland
properties.Description: The connection for the V1 and Bulimba Creek Bikeway requiring 7 road crossings is absurd. Both bikeways incredibly popular and well used by commuters and recreational cyclist alike, a better solution is sorely needed. This same connection is also required to continue on the Bulimba Creek bikeway too, a hard barrier for any young or casual cyclist.
To enter or exit the bikeway from this exit requires a very sharp angled turn and you always have to look out for cyclists speeding along the bikeway or down the exit.

Aberleigh Rd & the Inner Northern Bikeway are part of a main cycling route from the Northside to the city.  Cyclists need to ride on Herston Rd with no bike lane. Kerbside parking on Herston Rd combines with a narrow road carrying lots of traffic to create a dangerous zone where cyclists can be doored or hit by traffic overtaking aggresively.
Car drivers park here, even during the no parking period between 6-9am

No bicycle lane on this key bicycle route - forcing cyclists into busy single lane road. Southbound separated bikeway is on other side of the road when heading in a southerly direction - difficult to access in traffic. Poorly planned and desperately in need of improving.
No bike lanes, no safe crossing, parked cars, car dooring risk, poor road surface.  Terrible intersection also

Narrow blind corner on busy shared walkway from Suncorp Stadium to bicentennial bikeway. No visibility to oncoming pedestrians, cyclists of scooters turning the corner in either direction. Path often occluded by share e-bikes and e-scooters that have been dumped. Innumerable near misses.
Bike lanes are unfortunately full of parked cars at all times forcing cyclists into car lanes.
Bike lane ends on this busy uphill section of  Settlement Road.
No road shoulder,  it is the main bike route to the Gateway Bridge.
If you ride across the intersection going West, the bicycle lane is too narrow, and there is increased risk of being run off the road by a car or bus.
Cars parked in bike lane
Start and stop bike lanes all along Nudgee road. When lanes do exist, they're often blocked by parked cars. Expect to be weaving in and out of high speed heavy vehicle traffic.

This is a protected bike lane that crosses a road briefly with limited visibility. Cars are not looking for cyclists here. It could be improved by raising the cycle path and continuing it. Making it so cars cross a cycle path, not cyclists crossing a road.
At several points along Dornoch Tce, especially on the ascent when motor vehicle speed is much faster than cycle speed, there are pinch points where parked cars force a rider out into traffic.

Bike lane ends 20m before intersection and cars just squash up to the kerb. There is plenty of space to continue the bike lane and even put a bikebox infront of queued cars. Strangely, at some stage their even made the bikelane shorter - you can still see the old bike lane ends paint.

Uncontrolled turning from all directions, and a never-ending flow of heavy traffic, (drivers are never accommodating), mean crossing is an opportunistic multistep and often lengthy process that is not suitable for all ages and abilities. There are no reasonable alternative routes.
Bike lane ends with no facility to head south

This intersection is even worse heading west. If you stop at the lights, you have to restart on a deceptively steep incline, right next to cars lined up at the same lights, some of which will want to turn left directly in front of you, while you are distracted trying to clip in.
Constant dodging of pedestrians on footpath due to no cycling infrastructure. Quite narrow in certain locations.
High speed slip lane. Speed of vehicles and poor visibility (vehicles stopped on East-West Arterial Road) make it easy for drivers to not see cyclists travelling south on Nudgee road.
Poor visibility up to the right coming downhill at road crossing. Also, cars exiting quickly on the uphill lane.
Pick your poison - ride in the traffic and have cars aggressively beep at you, and 'punishment' pass you, or ride in the door-zone

Signal phasing sees right turning vehicles from Parkland Blvd turning at the same time the signals give a 'green man' for people crossing (foot or on bike) across Roma St. I've had multiple close passes  at this point - driver's not waiting for people to complete crossing .  Unsafe for children, tourists, visitors access precinct.
Car exiting side street pulled out in front of 2 of us and we hit the car. Both of us hospitalised with broken bones.

Oxlade Drive needs bike lanes linking New Farm Park to Merthyr Park. Commuters and recreational cyclists, including kids ride along here and there is no bike facilities at all. Car parking has been prioritised over cycle safety.
Cars in the right lane wave you through and particularly if an SUV or ute it is very hard to see the on-coming traffic in the left lane.
Just so vulnerable through busy cafe area with narrow bike lanes used for on-road vehicle parking.
Bake lane narrows significantly and cars constantly drift out of their lane
High use area for bikes  that has not been upgraded for decades.
Traffic changing lanes at intersection and no bike lanes
Bike lane in one direction ends. Bike lane in other direction turns into parking.
The bike lane narrows to a ridiculously small width right in front of a dangerous slip lane.

Griffith Street needs bike lanes linking Merthyr Park to the riverwalk. Commuters and recreational cyclists, including kids ride along here and there are no bike facilities at all. Car parking has been prioritised over cycle safety.
Many cars not indicating and turning through the bike lane without seeing the bikes and cutting off bikes

properties.Location: Kangaroo Point Cliffs Drive, Kangaroo Point, Queensland
properties.Description: This is a really popular bike and pedestrian route. It does have segregated bike and walking paths; but, they're extremely narrow. Bikes are squeezed between a slippery concrete curb and a waist high fence. Meanwhile, the road along here is very seldom used. It should be closed to all non-essential vehicles, and bikes should get the road space.
No cycling infrastructure. Footpath in the area often obstructed by works/businesses/club entry queues, and must be shared by pedestrians/cyclists/e-scooters moving in both directions.
Very busy industrial area, missing link to connect to bikeways at Murarrie recreation reserve and gateway bridge
There is no separation of traffic or bike lane
There is no good crossing of Sandgate road to get to Eagle Junction station, the NBB or Kedron Brook bikeway.

Where O'Connell crosses the rail line the bridge is too narrow and the road takes up almost the whole bridge width leaving no footpath on the south and a narrow path on the north. With traffic and a turn lane cycling here is scary. During Ekka, it is made even narrower and there is more car/cyclist conflict.

Sharp corner with impossible rear visibility for cyclists riding the river loop and coming off the cycle path space. Now has a "LOOK" painted sign on the road, but this is hard to do and is a cyclist death waiting to happen. This is part of the River Loop, the most popular cycle course in Brisbane. Desperately needs upgrade.

properties.Location: 632 Old Cleveland Road, Camp Hill, Queensland
properties.Description: No bike lane, narrow lanes with fast traffic
Cars accelerating meanwhile you need to be slowing to turn off the road

This intersection has been "upgraded" several times by Brisbane City Council misappropriating "black spot" funding, and each time they have made it more dangerous for cycling. Sir Fred Schonell Drive needs protected bike lanes the full length.
No bike lane. When stopped in left lane at a red light with green left arrow, drivers turning left often get aggressive towards cyclists who are stopped in the lane and going straight.

Graceville Ave around the Pamplett bridge area is narrow and twisting and there is no vehicle/bicycle/pedestrian separation.  The Pamphlett Bridge was built in the 1960's and the pavement has under a metre width for pedestrians and cyclists, this needs widening at the very least. Ideally separating all 3 forms would be the solution
This is the main North-South connector from the Kedron Brook Bikeway to the City.  Very narrow, cars parked in the Bikelane, high risk of dooring, poor motorist behaviour.  Basically a death trap.

Cars go way too fast here, I don't understand why this is a 60km rd when there are parts of nearby Beaudesert Rd with 4 lanes and no housing 50km. Dropping the speed limit and some painting (just the bicycle symbol not even a full bike lane) I think would help a lot.
Extremely busy intersection with no bike lanes to turn right. It's a highly popular bike route from Wynnum back to city

Corner is on the downhill from both approaches. Angle is too sharp and is a blind corner. Also has so many distractions (poles, signage, noisy road, cafe entry) for a shared path between riders and walkers. Needs slower speed signage.
A lot of bikes can be forced to prop on this very small traffic island in the middle of a very busy road during peak hours. Front or back wheels on recumbants or tandems could easily be clipped.
Whites Rd is the main way in and out of Lota. There is no bike lane or even shoulder to ride on and it is a 70km/h road. To avoid this road I take a much longer and hillier route on my commute.
Missing link between separated Downfall Creek bikeway and Gateway North bikeway. Many parked cars, high traffic and a lot of big trucks due to truck depots at end of Elliott Rd.

properties.Location: 135 Old Cleveland Road, Stones Corner, Queensland
properties.Description: Bike lane ends suddenly. Shortly after the road narrows and forces you into the traffic
The bike lane ends when it crosses the road, the signage is poor and inconsistent with other road crossings in the area
Outbound Old Cleveland Road going uphill, bike lane ends temporarily forcing rider close/into traffic. Traffic is fast moving and curb is commonly littered with road debris including glass.
The council has created a head-on situation between cars and bicycles by putting in a one-way chicane with no bicycle bypass.
High speed and high volume main road with no bicycle lane, but also no practical alternative route for cyclists due to a lack of crossings over the rail line funneling all traffic through here.
Dangerous road with no provision for bicycles
Needs painted bike lanes , not just cycling signs
driver behaviours & lack of awareness of cyclists & peds
This is a very busy intersection that regularly has a lot of cars queuing up. It can be very difficult to cross with cars darting in and out of the street and refusing to give way.

The road here is quite wide. Rat running means there is high traffic at inappropriate speeds. Parked cars make it difficult to see traffic for judging safe timing. Moving forward means starting on an uphill. Very difficult with children.
Too many cars no separated biking space.
Bike lane ends on Preston Rd, necessitating a merge. Cars are usually parked on the shoulder so can't use that. Vehicles often close pass to overtake.
Many drivers don't give way to cyclists travelling north on Worrell St
entering and exiting cars with cyclists and pedestrians. seen a few accidents

Bike lane here narrows a LOT. Down to almost nothing- see attached pic. Sharing road with heavy vehicles and high speed, high volume traffic is very sketchy especially near rush hour and school pick-ups.

V1 is generally excellent, but the couple of km through woolangabba to the river is narrow, twisty, has quite a few hills, and quite busy. Widening and straightening the path would improve safety and travel speed.

Nudgee Road from the M7 to Kingsford Smith Drive is problematic in both directions, the speed limit should be 50, there are only intermittent bike lanes, cars parked in bike lanes, and as it's a through road is very busy at peak times.

properties.Location: 1135 Logan Road, Holland Park West, Queensland
properties.Description: Car parking is priorities over cyclists safety.
New railway station development has made this area more unsafe for cyclists and pedestrians.
There's only a footpath on one side of the road heading towards Aherwood.

Coming off the bike / walking path on the eastern side of the Story Bridge (going from New Farm towards Kangaroo Point), the bike lane ends and cyclists need to make a blind left turn onto Deakin Street which is a busy street used by cars.

properties.Location: 153 Sir Samuel Griffith Drive, Bardon, Queensland
properties.Description: The Ithaca Ck bikeway ends here, with no link or further bikeway around Mt Cootha, one of the most popular cycle spots (on and off road) in Brisbane.

properties.Location: 89 Seventeen Mile Rocks Road, Oxley, Queensland
properties.Description: The roundabout was recently redesigned and BCC town planners added very confusing lane markings and abruptly end the bicycle lane

properties.Location: 4 Seventeen Mile Rocks Road, Oxley, Queensland
properties.Description: The road has two blind corners and is a major car/truck thoroughfare. The surface is also in a poor state
cyclists are forced to ride in the door zone
Cracknell Rd is the main access route for many Annerley / Tarragindi residents but it's extremely dangerous, with 60km/hr speed limit, on-street parking on both sides and no bike lanes at all.
Ithaca Cr Bikeway simply ends at Carwoola St intersection, rather thank linking to Simpsons Rd / Mt Cootha. High speed traffic.
very frequent occurrence of taxi drivers standing in the bicycle lane, or opening their doors onto oncoming cyclists.  There is no buffer zone between parked taxis and the cycle lane.
High number of vehicles, lots of cars parked on the side of the road with doors opening. Unsafe for people cycling or school kids.

Very dangerous during school pickup/drop off hours. Cars regularly stack up on Gladstone RD because of the lights and people will pull across the keep clear trying to get into the school/pope st. Was T-Boned by a car when coming down the hill, and you see regular near misses with commuters.

Pedestrians who stand on the riders lane, or attempt to cross on the pedestrian 'don't walk' signal without checking the riders lane.

I've had numerous near misses here, including one soft collision.
High traffic area during peak hours. Requires cyclists to cross four lanes (plus turning slip lane) of traffic.

This crossing of Lamington Avenue is on the main connection between the Lutwyche Busway Station underpass and Kedron Brook. It is poorly designed, with people cycling forced to ride in front of traffic in and out of Lamington onto Bradshaw Street. There needs to be a proper raised priority crossing.
Cars dive into parks, come out of parks, narrow, cars block cycleway
Why do we provide for free parking for workers along here and effectively make cycling along here more difficult and dangerous?  This is a key link in the River Loop.   Fix it BCC.

Along this section the no standing zone on the outbound side is 4-7pm. Further along in Tingalpa the no standing zone is 3-6pm. Why the disparity? This road is busy a lot of the time. Why not unify both sections to be no standing 3-7pm?
Even though there is a bicycle lane, drivers drive very close to the bicycle lane esp trucks
One of the most trafficked cycling routes in Brisbane as part of the River Loop. No bike lanes. Have seen so much poor driver behaviour along this stretch.
Bicycle lane shared with pedestrians and surface isn't smooth. Footpath is next to a very busy road.

properties.Location: 513a Seventeen Mile Rocks Road, Seventeen Mile Rocks, Queensland
properties.Description: Trying to get into Edenbrooke Drive from Seventeen Mile Rocks Road is extremely hazardous. The bike lane ends just after Boyland Street and you have to cross effective two lanes of 60kph traffic to get into the right turn lane.
While Herston Road technically has a bike lane, it's all door zone. Once cars are parked, the bike lane is too narrow for a bike.

Crest of a hill on arterial road, squeezed between traffic and barrier, often fallen branches too. Pinjarra Rd is safer than Moggill Rd (although steep) but to get onto it you need to get past this spot. To go towards Bellbowrie /Moggill you can alternate sides of Moggill Rd with paths, there are no other alternatives

A connecting path through Henry Clarkson Park around the back of Kenmore Sth State School to Glenfrew st would be a useful addition for school kids  and cyclists connecting to the freeway path via Akuna st. Bikes can then avoid Aberfoyle st which is narrow when travelling up to Kenmore rd and the Kenmore high school & Our Lady of the Rosary school

properties.Location: Sir Samuel Griffith Drive, Mount Coot-Tha, Queensland
properties.Description: High speed vehicles and slow bikes going up the hill. Adding in concrete islands, bollards, or any other measure to protect the bikelane would resolve this issue. It would also discourage the late night hoons which frequent this area as they will use the bikelane to go faster around corners.
No kerb ramps on either side of the crossing or in the median. Poor infrastructure for cyclists or any wheeled user or person with a pram, mobility device etc
cars parked in the bike lane makes riding in the bike lane dangerous.
Bike lane ends forcing bikes onto road or path

This narrow shared path is the route between the underpass at Lutwyche Busway Station and the Kedron Brook Bikeway. Half way down there is a concealed entrance which is the main access to the apartment building next to the path. This is very dangerous for people cycling and walking.

Road narrows at this point and shoulder disappears, while cars often remain parked along edge. This creates a pinch point and cars often pass at unsafe distances. No alternative cycling infrastructure to traverse to riverside bikeway.

properties.Location: 288 Pine Mountain Road, Mount Gravatt East, Queensland
properties.Description:
The road is too narrow and it appears there is some ground movement on the bank of the road. This making traffic changing lanes in order to avoid the road imperfections.
Please note there are no other road alternatives near by that connects carindale bike way with Holland Park or Cav Road.
No dedicated space for riders on this signed route makes it especially dangerous for riders going up the steep hill

It's not a bike lane but the shoulder becomes really narrow because of the driveways. There is also a man hole there. So you're basically left with a choice to thread the needle between the manhole and driveway or merge into traffic.
Previous death, fast cars, no shoulder
narrow road, no shoulder
Kerbside parking is a hazard for people riding bicycles.

This corner of Park & Milton is not designed for cyclists. The pedestrian crossing wait time is very long, if you're not on the road you're stuck on a very narrow corner, cars are flying past both roads. It's only safe if you're already part of the road traffic.

properties.Location: 380 Mount Samson Road, Armstrong Creek, Queensland
properties.Description: Fast traffic with narrow edges
Very narrow bridge which you are forced onto the road of as the bike shoulder ends. That combined with the steep incline makes it very stressful. The footpath is also very narrow.

On this stretch of road on the west side, there is more than enough room to install a protected lane near the kerb, with the parked cars between the protected land and the road.
There is zero infrastructure on the east side of the road.

properties.Location: 18 Wights Mountain Road, Samford Valley, Queensland
properties.Description: A difficult intersection to share with traffic and little alternative. Some drivers are patient but others downright aggressive.

Cars want to turn left into Benett St while cyclists are usually continuing straight.  Tough instersections for cars to give way.  Also very hard for cars to turn right from Bennett St into Sylvan Rd.  I always expect cars to not see me here.

Speed needs to be reduced to 50kph as it is a vital connecting road between the residential area and Western Freeway Bikeway from Julie Rd and the schools and shopping centre on Grand Ave.
It's crazy to have painted cycling signs in the middle of each lane direction to indicate it's a shared space, while the speed limit is 60kph.
High speed dangerous road crossing of a shared path.
This would be a great way to get off Moggill Rd, but is hard to access with the speed of the traffic

The bike lane ends and riders are forced onto shared lanes that are narrowing down to the rail bridge. The footpath quickly narrows and goes right past a blind entry to Dutton Park Station then round a blind corner into Kent St.

Driveway to complex (including two gyms) is blind from the northern approach and sees a lot of vehicles entering and exiting. Currently no signage to indicate that the shared path is a shared path, so little interest from motorists to look or give way to uses of the path.

No safe way to cycle eastbound on Moggill Rd - there is a 'No bicycles' sign on the footpath, no mared cycle lane on Moggill Rd, side road for the student houses is narrow, full of parked cars and stops before the Russell Lane traffic lights. After traffic lights there's no safe way past the bus stop without conflicting with bus passengers
Too much traffic and bad driver behaviour make a stretch around schools unsafe. Needs a separated bikeway!

No cycling infrastructure and narrow footpaths with many residential driveways makes it unsafe trying to access Sunnybank Plaza from the South. And there aren't any viable alternate routes through residential streets.
Brisbane City Council allow cars to park in the bicycle lanes on Nudgee Rd which is dangerous for people riding in the bicycle lanes.
Very little shoulder after the bike lane just ends
High traffic area along Grey St, lots of parked cars with people frequently getting in and out

Crazy place to allow a coffee shop to operate! No safe stopping place on a glide off. Anyone indicating to turn appears they are taking the glide off, then brake suddenly and pull over to the coffee shop!
Zero shoulder for cycling and the footpath alternative is narrow along Samford rd

No safe/legal way northbound on Albert St, across Turbot St at lights in evening peak to get from one bike zone to the other. Footpath full of pedestrians and right turn only from Albert St. This intersection always forces bikes to cross dangerously as there is no other option

This crossing is very dangerous and it is a major path for kids going to local schools and daycare. Lower speed is required here with red light camera to ensure vehicles stop at the traffic light so to avoid run over pedestrian and cyclists crossing the road. Many near misses here.

The bike lane becomes really narrow. There is a drain right on the apex of the bend, which makes the usable width of the bike lane really narrow. Being a corner cars often cross into the bike lane making it extremely hazardous.
This is a narrow road with no bitumen outside white lines
Brisbane St between Hooper St and Limestone St is dual carriageway in both directions with the gutter as the only shoulder. Commuting can be dangerous on this stretch.
Narrow
Sylvan Road is the main connector between the Centenary and Bicentennial Bikeways. Carries 3000+ cyclists per day. NO protected bike lanes, and parked cars make sight lines at side streets poor.

Bowhill Rd is dangerous for cycling, with zero infrastructure (lacks even a shoulder or footpath), a narrow bridge on a blind curve and trucks travelling at 70 km/h.

There are zero safe east-west corridors in this area, meaning most cyclists travelling from south-western suburbs to southern suburbs must take an extremely long detour to Ipswich Rd.
No safe cycling infrastructure provided on road. Consistently told to get off footpath by pedestrians. Mayham during game days when crowds take up all space on footpath.
Ann St in general is quite unsafe and needs a dedicated bike lane
This intersection was redesigned by the Labor State Government with no provision on Nudgee Rd for people on bicycles. Prior to the upgrade you could ride on the painted chevrons.
When cycling here after dark, when heading south, the road lights shining over the top of the wall provide a lot of glare and make it very difficult to see where you are going.
Car doors opening/cars pulling out and tree roots creating uneven surface
The footpath to road transition is damaged and there is a broken concrete surface. Very unsafe to cross on road bicycle. It used to have a plastic cover for a couple years, but then it went missing.
No visibility when trying to cross from Thornton St to the west side of storey Bridge. Cars come off the main street too fast. Needs to be a safe place to cross.

Despite being a quiet backstreet, it is wide and designed for speeding to feel comfortable. Drivers sometimes use it at high-speed to bypass traffic jams on the adjacent Stafford Rd. Needs traffic calming.
Busy Lytton Rd the marked bike lanes end and meets a 2-lane narrow bridge with zero shoulder with lots of heavy truck traffic
There is no bike lane and cars pass far too closely to bike riders. This os a very big, busy intersection that requires a bike lane for safety

This is a shared path from the south end start, up to Bakery Rd.
Racing/speeding cyclists make this unsafe for casual riders and pedestrians, especially on the down hills where these speeding cyclist could be passing at double your speed.
We need state legislation for cycling speed limits. If cyclists want to race, they can go to a track.

Both directions are problematic.  This intersection doesn't suit the amount of bike and cars that travel through here every day.  It's hard to get across Sylvan Rd at the cross point so sometime I head up to Norwood St but leads to problems with cyclists heading outbound.
Kerb suddenly juts out into road where a centre traffic island has been installed. Cars are too impatient to wait behind a cyclist and there isn't enough room to pass.

Narrow wooden footbridge over creek, too narrow for bikes to pass pedestrians or for bikes going in opposite directions. Poor visibility from both directions means you can't see the path on the other side of the bridge before reaching the bridge.
Roundabout has been removed and motor vehicles are driven by aggressive people.

properties.Location: 133 Spring Mountain Boulevard, Spring Mountain, Queensland
properties.Description: The bike lane ends into a row of parked cars with a median strip which means cars can't overtake safely

the northbound transition for cyclists traveling to Uxbridge St offers no protection.  This newly "upgraded" intersection installed a slip lane.  This is not safe systems infrastructure.  This is not something an interested but concerned cyclist would feel comfortable riding on.

Vehicles often run the red light turning right from Days Rd onto Grange Rd, and also continuing east through the intersection on Days Rd. NE kerb ramp of the ped crossing is very steep and the corner is blind. Risk of school children being hit by vehicles.

There is no quality north-south route from Enogerra Ck bikeway for CHILDREN to ride (or walk) to school. Our kids walk to school (30mins each way), but want to ride - the footpath is poor, lack of separation from 60km/h+ cars on arterial road.  Rubbish bins, parked cars on footpath, broken pavement, unsafe side streets.
The cycleway connecting to up to Ipswitch Rd ends here and riders have to either ride amongst traffic  or use narrow footpaths to Marshall Rd

The westbound bicycle lane ends here, pushing riders to merge with motor vehicles at a crossing and intersections. Riders are then forced to ride in the door zone next to parked cars and 50km/h traffic
There are marked bike lanes here but also car parking that takes up all the bike lane. They effectively don't exist on a skinny road.

On road car parking with narrow on road bike lane and higher speed traffic makes it tricky to navigate safely, regardless of time of day. The road surface has lifted in sections due to tree roots which are difficult to ride around due to the nature of traffic in the area

The high volume of school kids and drop offs and constant heavy peak hour traffic and a set of traffic lights with short "walk" times to get access to the cycleway and public transport is a nightmare. Surely building a tunnel from the cycleway and school underneath Fairfield road to Peter Doherty St would reduce risk to peds and cyclists

western approach signal phasing can leave road riding cyclist turning right from the kerbside (shared thru and R turn)  lane exposed to potential rear end crash from through running motorists - see attached photo.  R turning bike has red arrow, driver in kerbside lane has green lantern.  At night the cyclist will be less visible
There is a lot of traffic on Simpsons/Stuardholme road, and the traffic island is insufficiently wide to cross with a bike (wheels stick out either side of island) from Boundary road.
Added slip lane makes this dangerous for cyclists
Cars on turning there at 50-60 km/hr while this is the access to the city from the story bridge pedestrian and cycle lane. I had and seen many near misses

An uncontrolled intersection with no bicycle priority or bicycle phase can be difficult to cross in busy times. Trying to turn right from Rode Rd into Bundal St is very stressful if waiting for eastbound traffic to clear while westbound traffic skirts around you.
During peak times cars park in cycling lane leaving next to no space left for cyclist.

Alignment of the road curves to the left, so drivers stay as close the left side of the road as they navigate the bend. However, the shoulder/ bike lane disappears to almost nothing at het apex of the curve and drivers do no slow down for you to pass this point before passing. Many drivers encroach into the parking lane.
The path is too narrow for two-way bicycle and pedestrian travel, and in the middle of the corner are two unsafe utilities covers
This whole road has little to no shoulder. Foot path is uneven so it's not an option. Use this road carefully and avoid around peak hrs.

No bike lanes, heavy trucks and narrow lanes. Terrible pedestrian path which is not continuous along either side of the road. Ultimately is a really good road connecting bikeway to suburbs and should be improved to prevent accidents.
Pedestrian island causes a pinch point. There's a large speed differential between cars and bikes in the uphill direction. Many vehicles will close pass to get through the pinch point.

I feel unsafe riding along Lomandra Drive because of the large number of trucks that use this road. There is also a large drop off on the edge of the road after the resurfacing.
There is plenty of room for a dedicated bike path seperate from the road.
Drivers tend to go into the bus lane early up the hill while you are cycling up the hill.
Cars turning left encroach on the bike lane.
This is marked as a cycle route, but there there is no cycling infrastructure and motorist don't give way.
Cars will often close pass to get ahead of cyclists leading into the round about.
Footpath narrow and uneven. No bike path.
The cycling infrastructure has made the intersection feel unsafe.

Due to lack of maintenance, the grass has grown across the bikeway, reducing its width significantly. While the verges are routinely mowed, the edges are not managed. This issue is evident at multiple locations along the path from Toombul to the golf course. It creates a passing challenge for cyclists.
It makes you lame split.
The is no CityLink Cycleway here, the map is lying to us. It is just a construction site where you need to dodge busses and avoid pedestrians.
Road narrows, shoulder disappears between School rd and Moggill Rd, creating pinch point, which leads to drivers passing at unsafe distances.
To follow the cycle path between Kokoda St and the Centenary bikeway, you have to cross the road at a blind corner with little visibility of traffic in either direction
This is a busy road with and is a direct route to Racecourse road and then onto the bike path at Kingsford smith drive and into the city. It is a rough road forcing cyclists onto the road
Large amount of sand on the path makes it a crash risk.
Drivers almost never give way to peds and cyclists at the southbound slip lane (Ipswich Rd / Ekibin Rd). They take the turn very fast thanks to the very wide lanes and road design.
Very narrow, high speed road with no footpath on the left hand side, means cyclists need to ride on the road. Very dangerous crest.
Very important access to the Centenary Bikeway for residents in Jindalee, and access to Centenary State high School. No bike lanes, but plenty of space for "on street parking".

Cars are often parked forcing riders into the lane and drivers attempt to overtake around the blind corner. I've encountered several near crashes here as drivers attempt dangerous impatient maneuvers.

Large volume of cars crossing painted bike lane to enter / leave driveways along this section of Sandgate road. Cars will often misjudge the speed of cyclists, cutting you off as they cross the lane to enter driveways.

Sinnathamby has wide bike lanes , but no protection on a 70kmh road (more of a shoulder than a bike lane). Much of the road doesn't even have a footpath on one side. Bridges do have footpaths and kerb ramps but aren't ideal
Road narrow under bridge and two lanes merge into one lane. There are not too many alternatives to cross rail line.

High speed slip lane with high heavy vehicle traffic that crosses a painted bike lane to join Sandgate Road. At the speed that vehicles can carry through here, drivers could easily not see a cyclist and any collision would be significant

Shortest bicycle lane ever forces riders into far left position entering an intersection where drivers cannot see them over the rise. The bike lane ends at the intersection as drivers exiting Clarence Rd attempt to cross into the riders path.
Difficult to get across this street when riding with kids.
This is the main place kids cross to get to Yeronga State School on the south side. Main road 60 kph each way without any crossing facilities, but still safer than the roundabout down the hill.

properties.Location: 34 Lower Clifton Terrace, Red Hill, Queensland
properties.Description: A very narrow spot with multiple road user types converging in awkward geometry.
Bike lane suddenly disappears, traffic through the intersection is squeezed while cross traffic is trying to merge

This a key cycling connection to west end, too unsafe for most. It is also opposite a train station that is barricaded to pedestrians - too unsafe to cross a road to a train station. Rather than make it safe, they curtail pedestrians....
Busy traffic area and cars are always parked in the bike lane. It's also very near a train station which make it a high danger area for car-dooring.
Kedron Brook Road is a key cycling link between the Kedron Brook bike path and the CBD.  It has poor cycling infrastructure with high risk of car dooring due to shops.

The cycle lane ends on the northern approach to this intersection, forcing cyclists to take the lane which has busy traffic travelling at speed. A significant proportion of motorists turn left into the left slip-lane without giving way to cyclists riding north onto Parton Street (which has priority).

Difficult to merge off the go between onto the cycle lane as no visibility to the left. You need to come to a complete halt to see properly, which most people don't do. Seems like a mirror here would help the situation
Car drivers routinely fail to give way to bicycle riders already on the roundabout
When riding to The Gap from the city in the afternoon/evening, there is no safe way to cross Payne Road after crossing Waterworks Rd at the traffic lights.
Buses block the view of impatient drivers who pull into traffic without seeing bikes. Visibility for drivers coming out of Burbing is poor
There a poor sight lines and drivers don't always stop at the stop sign and don't look for bikes coming inbound on Hudson Road.
Cyclists and pedestrians are crammed together with cars speeding past. Feels very vulnerable

This spot is on the main bikeway heading north. There can be a lot of vehicle traffic and there's a wall making visibility impossible to see footpath traffic. A raised bikeway crossing would be ideal here to give priority to the bikeway.
Cars proceeding through the intersection squeeze cyclists into parked cars

Need to travel along Earl St to acces the Veloway from the northern end of Annerley. There are no line markings for bikes. Trucks park along the side of the road making the gap narrow for cars travelling along this section. The pedestrian island creates a narrow section of road causing another conflict zone for cyclists and cars
Limited connections along Downfall Creek forces bikes onto Webster Road. Have to navigate a large round about and cross multiple lanes of traffic to connect up to the next stretch.
Bike lane ends abruptly before roundabout and funnels rider into traffic

Shaw Rd is a significant connection due to the Kedron Brook bikeway and the bike lane/shoulder available towards the North Brisbane Bikeway, if coming from Wavell Heights or Bilson Rd. The road here is up hill so travel on a bike is slow, however there is no shoulder and the speed differential with passing cars is high.

properties.Location: 470 Upper Edward Street, Spring Hill, Queensland
properties.Description: This is the recommended route from Victoria park into the city by bike but it is not safe. There are so many cars and buses, no real cycle lanes. No one is looking for cyclists.

At this point on Riding Rd there are usually parked cars and bikes are forced out into the traffic lane. With cars parked on both sides of this busy road there is no way for cars to pass bicycles safely.
Bike lane painted on road but bus comes across it to stop at bus stop

Traffic island for the slip lane narrows the left lane creating a pinch point for cyclists continuing straight on Manly Rd. Motorists frequently close pass cyclists at this intersection as they don't account for the narrowing of the lane.

The footpath over the bridge is deeply unpleasant to use - it's extremely narrow (made worse by overgrown trees right next to it), has a poor surface and the connections either side are poorly designed. The road isn't a great option either as it has no shoulder and is often busy with heavy vehicle traffic.
Going slightly uphill and needing to takeoff from lights. Lots of traffic mixed with parked cars out of hours

This is the crest of a rather big hill, it is reasonably narrow and a residential street that has become a rat run since the giveaway sign / roundabout has been removed at the intersection of Homestead and Tarragindi Road.
lanes are narrow and we transition from having a cycle lane to having nothing; high traffic area with a bus stop.
The bike lanes is sandwiched between two lanes of motor vehicle traffic.

Vehicle speeds are very high through this large radius slip lane and almost nobody gives way to pedestrians or cyclists. With a school just a few hundred meters along California Creek Road traffic lights are a MUST on this slip lane.
To go straight you need to go into the main lane, and there has been a critical accident with a cyclist with this exact spot from cars not looking before turning off the 80km road to the side road.
Narrow road over 2 bridges poor shoulder surface and road so narrow it's impossible for vehicles especially large to yield the metre distance
You have to be very aware of the traffic behaviour, as the road goes from a bike lane, to no lane.
Road works have made the road unsafe for bikes
Busses and cars up my ass going around the corner
Outbound Winn road to Clear mountain/ Samford. Intersection on bend with gutter drop off and no escape for cars passing turning traffic. Fortunately the road is not too busy early mornings
Cyclist two a breast on single bike lane
to narrow parked cars and lanes disappear into the gutter. on the west bound side. east side there is lots of room. I ride on the footpath

properties.Location: 347a Albany Creek Road, Bridgeman Downs, Queensland
properties.Description: The bike lane just disappears after the intersection and bikes have to merge into the left car lane. There is only about 20 metres and the the road widens again
Cars entering freeway, and bike trying to turn left onto the V1 bike way. It is worse leaving the V1 and trying to go into the University.

Very narrow / weaving access to this creek crossing. The signage (warning) may be missed by little riders/kids - and you can see that the bollards in place have been knocked over many times.  The risk of someone launching themselves into the rocks of the creek is real due to the gradient and the sharp corner turn.

Brisbane Valley Rail Trail very popular with cyclists and hikers, but there is no safe crossing of the Brisbane Valley Hwy. No traffic island, no protection.  Vehicles don't want to slow down for cycles (or hikers). Continuous traffic means you have to wait ages to get over the road. Vehicles travel too fast because road is straight.

If you are on a bike trying to turn right onto Sportstar Dr, traffic lights WILL NOT turn green as a bike isn't heavy enough for the pressure sensor. There is no button for cyclists to press to activate the lights.
no bike lanes in high traffic road

properties.Location: 138 Springfield Lakes Boulevard, Springfield Lakes, Queensland
properties.Description: Critical link for residents going to school or to Orion. Has some bike lanes but they end at speed-prioritised roundabouts. Between Summit Dr and Springfield Pkwy has no bike infrastructure
There are lots of parked cars coming up to the roundabout. When you enter the lane cars attempt to overtake to get in front prior to entering the roundabout.
Aggressive behaviour from drivers

No good cycling infrastructure other than the River loop through Chelmer - Oxley. Sharrows take you along a winding and hilly route which doesn't go to many points of interest or use. Oxley Road or another parallel alignment needs bicycle lanes.
As the road goes around the corner it narrows. Drivers can't see this and often attempt to overtake around the corner ending up way too close
Busy road as people cut through side streets to avoid the intersection Oxley Rd/Sherwood Rd. Lots of school traffic.
Lots of through traffic due to people avoiding intersections and safe traffic/time.

There is in theory a wide shoulder and bicycle markings. However, that goes out the door during school drop-off. Where cars stop on yellow lines, double park, open doors into the path of approaching traffic, fail to indicate, make illegal u-turns over solid white centre lines, drive straddling the lane/shoulder marking.
Bike lanes end, no shoulder, forced to merge in front of cars. This is One of the only routes available to cross Scrubby Creek.

properties.Location: 347a Albany Creek Road, Bridgeman Downs, Queensland
properties.Description: Travel in a westerly direction on Albany Creek Rd, there is no bicycle lane option to call for a right turn into Beckett Road.
Difficult and dangerous to turn right outbound when trying to get off too-busy Oxley Rd

At the south end of the Bocce Club building there is an improvised bike thoroughfare from the International City Church carpark.  Bikes using this improvised thoroughfare create what is effectively an unofficial intersection where they cross the main path.  This unofficial intersection is a blind intersection for southbound riders.
Drivers often do not look for cyclists despite the Centenery bikeway emptying onto this street.

Cars do not look right when turning left
Had many near misses
Light sensor does not detect a bike, leaving you stranded in the right-turn lane unless you proceed against a red arrow.

Drivers speed out of the petrol station to beat traffic, not thinking about the pedestrian crossing. The flashing lights on the "give way to pedestrians" sign start flashing way too late to warn drivers. Also, the sign is in the opposite direction to where car drivers are actually concentrating.

This intersection is very difficult to navigate for anyone who is travelling by active transport. Turning right from Baroona Road onto Nash Street can be very intimidating when dealing with motor traffic who are wanting to speed up and continue straight. There is no provisions for walking and cycling and a pedestrian crossing was not delivered.
No safe way for kids to cross to the other side to reach local primary schools (bike path ends, road is very busy with school vehicle traffic)

Inbound on Wynnum Rd between Joe Lambert Dr and Stanton Rd the bike lane is quite narrow. But there are numerous places where stormwater grates make the bike lane effectively only a few centimetres wide.
This section of bike path is not clear, not wide and people don't care about cyclists when taking the corner where hardwood St starts. All in all a dangerous spot that should at least be looked at.
Large shrub / vegetation extends into road corridor

This street is on the Principal Cycle Network, is uphill, and at this point there is a blind crest with following cars regularly overtaking without the ability to see approaching traffic. It is necessary to either remove on-street parking (and make it a safe cycle street) or install a median so vehicles cannot overtake.

This street is on the Principal Cycle Network, is uphill, and at this point there is another blind crest with following cars regularly overtaking without the ability to see approaching traffic. It is necessary to either remove on-street parking (and make it a safe cycle street) or install a median so vehicles cannot overtake.
RK
Narrowing road around corner with limited visibility,  drivers often attempt overtake as lane narrows and conflict with cyclists.

properties.Location: 236b Old Cleveland Road East, Capalaba, Queensland
properties.Description: No safe bike lanes at intersection
High speed section, no alternatives, high level of dangerous driving displayed, no protection or space given.

This whole section from when you leave Stage 3 of the NBB at Price St until you reach north of Murphy Rd is terrible. Lots of twists and turns, hard to navigate, crossing busy roads with no priority or with slow multi-stage crossings, many sections shared with fast-moving traffic. The whole section needs careful new design.

This whole section from when you leave Stage 3 of the NBB at Price St until you reach north of Murphy Rd is terrible. Lots of twists and turns, hard to navigate, crossing busy roads with no priority or with slow multi-stage crossings, many sections shared with fast-moving traffic. The whole section needs careful new design.
There is a parking lane with a yellow line along it so cars cannot park there. If cars can't park there, why not use it for separated bike lanes instead?
Busy road, cars do not leave 1m gap, lots of parked cars

Cars turning right onto Logan Rd (from Birdwood) then left onto Abbotsleigh St like to speed past cyclists going straight on Logan Rd to then turn left in front of them rather than patiently wait behind the cyclist
pinch points between traffic flow and parked cars, both ways. Add speeding and tradies in utes and it is a less than favourable spot to ride a bicycle.
Drivers exiting Regatta hotel without looking for cyclist coming off Coronation Drive cycleway

I ride in the marked cycle lane to ensure I don't spend 75% of the light cycle waiting to cross Chalk. Those turning R into Fraser St turn without yielding (esp. if traffic is queued across intersection), those turning out of Fraser St do not stop behind the stop line, and drivers drive in bike lane to get around cars queued turning R into Chalk St
Drivers constantly drive through red lights

The exit to the roundabout narrows into one lane with a barrier on the left and a raised island divider on the right. Cars attempting to overtake while a cyclist exits the roundabout will cut off the cyclist and cause a hazardous condition.

The agency responsible for this section of path has installed grey paint squares  that is very slippery in the wet (underneath the slightly less slippery white bicycle and arrow). It's disconcerting when your front or rear wheel slips out
Overhanging fig trees deposit debris on the path which becomes incredibly slippery when wet.
The shoulder is covered in rubbish, sticks, glass, rivets, etc. along the entire length of this 90km/h stretch of road.

There is a steep rise in the path leading up to the pedestrian button, and it's already on a hill. Coming up from Ifield Street it is difficult to get a heavy bike close enough to the pedestrian button to push it, meaning I have to leave my bike basically in the road shoulder, walk over and push the button, then cross with the lights.
Most traffic is heading North on the Bruce highway whilst cyclists are heading East
Footpath is blocked by tree, and the whole road has too many aggressive car drivers.

Steep uphill, narrow road, poor visibility over the crest - motorists do not want to vere onto the other side of the road to go around a cyclist - so they squeeze past and push the rider into the door zone.
v

This roundabout, on what should be a quiet street, is designed so both car and bus drivers can comfortably fly through at 60km/h. When bus drivers speed through without looking for people already on the roundabout, you know the design of the roundabout is wrong, and definitely not safe for bike riders and pedestrians.
I don't feel safe when it's dark.  There is inadequate lighting

properties.Location: Mount Nebo Road, Enoggera Reservoir, Queensland
properties.Description: This is a beautiful climb, but the behaviour of car drivers and motorbike riders is absolutely appalling. People in groups clearly trying to impress each other with their reckless driving, speeding, and close passes.
Because they have bigger and bigger cars they residents have total disrespect for rules and safety

properties.Location: 40 Point O'halloran Road, Victoria Point, Queensland
properties.Description: I ride in a small group of approx 6 people of elderly age, continually cars come close here as no bike lane despite other sections of the road with a bike lane. Road itself is quite narrow and it is a main access road.

bike lane ends just before round about and starts again after round about, this is a large round about with poor visibility, this is a dual lane round about in a 60kph zone and traffic passes cyclists very close and fast.

Bike lane ends and goes onto a very narrow section of Graham Rd. There is a wide grassed shoulder that could be used to widen the road and add a cycling lane along Graham Rd, which is very wide. This is the main route to access the Downfall Creek cycling paths from Bridgeman Downs. Lack of bike lanes is significant oversight!
Separated bike lane randomly stops for no reason

Very narrow section of road shoulder covered with dropped Mango tree branches and fruit. No real room to safely dodge surface hazards without joining traffic stream (uphill, 70kph limit). Road authorities are aware of the hazard, as they have put in signage (see attached photo)

This road leads down to the Downfall Creek cycling pathways. There is no turn right lane or bike lanes. It's a dangerous turn as a lot of traffic comes up from Gympie Road, and sitting in the middle of the road trying to turn right is quite dangerous.
Zero margin for error here - use Kilkivan Ave to stay alive
Heavily trafficked bridge crossing

properties.Location: 424 Old Cleveland Road, Camp Hill, Queensland
properties.Description: Outbound- Bike line prior to the Bennets road intersection but not directly afterwards while the road narrows
Cars travel too fast down here - but it's a great connection to the bikepath and there's yellow bikes on the road.
Cars turning right from Parkland Drive, across the dedicated bikway, will jump red lights.
Separate bike lanes ends and throws you out onto 4 lane busy road, wrong side of road. Narrow entry on/off road to bike lane. Poorly thought out, on one of the most frequently used paths in Brisbane.

properties.Location: North Star Crossing, Springfield Central, Queensland
properties.Description: If you are on a bike in the Australia Ave cycle lane, coming from the station and trying to turn right, traffic lights WILL NOT turn green as a bike isn't heavy enough for the pressure sensor. There is no button for cyclists to press to activate the lights.

This busy roundabout is at a crest and traffic from any direction does not have clear sight line to any other direction until they actually enter the roundabout. Part of the reason for poor line of site is because the roundabout is at a crest, but planting within roundabout also obstructs sight lines.

If you are on a bike trying to turn right from Grande Ave onto Sportstar Dr, right-turn-arrow traffic lights WILL NOT turn green as a bike isn't heavy enough for the pressure sensor. There is no button for cyclists to press to activate the lights.
Very risky when cycling from dirt track into Lyndale Rd. Traffic coming from Honeyeater Dr has no warning of this concealed driveway and visibility is obscured by trees and bushes.
Recently added pedestrian crossing

Narrow bridge - the only way to link up the paths. The dangerous part is relatively blind spot pushing you out on road [south bound] + if ever 2 bunches meet - with a walker + their dog - we will see carnage. Definitely asking for a split path or wider bridge. Often get abused by walkers as well..don't blame them...but Strava segments don't wait.
Lots of trucks going past to the quarry

This 300m section of Bunker Rd. is narrow with a very rough surface. Speed limit is 80kph. The edges are broken and pot holed with no shoulder. The remainder of Bunker Rd. is wide smooth hot mix with generous shoulders and a speed limit of 60kph. Bunker Road is a busy commuting thoroughfare. Cyclists are forced into a very dangerous situation.
No bike lane or footpath and plenty of cars. Road narrows as it crosses bridge.
Narrowness of path
Near misses as no bike lane
The shoulder here is very narrow and the road is heavily trafficked. This bridge always feels unsafe.

The bike path takes many tight twists and turns and there is a lot of tall grass, making it impossible to see where the path goes in many places and to pass pedestrians or othe bikes. I was relieved to not encounter other bikes on my trip 24/01/2024. Path seriously need grass to me trimmed from edges.
Slippery, blind narrow corner on a very very popular route. Really should be fixed up.

Very busy intersection at peak times. On the main bikeway [road] section for larger groups avoiding the winding path along brook. Driver seem to flow around the corner continuously - in both directions. Be quick or be hit and call it loud.
Cars can turn into side streets from Tingal Road too fast due to no physical measure to manage turning speed across the bike lane.
A very narrow section of Honour Ave with vehicle and pedestrian movement rushing to coffee shop / food venues

Despite being a massive high school with a couple of thousand students, the road is designed for cars and cars only. Perhaps remove parking from one side to create a separated bike lane. The wide footpath is useless for cycling when it has a couple of thousand kids legally using it as a footpath.

There is no island crossing on this whole stretch making it difficult to get to the shopping centre from the west. Traffic become very busy making it dangerous for both pedestrians and cyclists. The only alternative is to cross at old Cleveland road which is not great for those with mobility issues.

This neighbourhood street forms part of the Ennogera Creek Bikeway, but also a major rat run and drag strip for some drivers. The street carries 5000 cars per day which is excessive for a neighbourhood street. Some drivers put walkers and riders under pressure and stress. This should be a haven for 8 to 80 ages to enjoy safe cycling.

This neighbourhood street is a major rat run. There is no quiet route to the local shops. Some drivers will drive very aggressively with close pass and dangerous overtaking. This should be a haven for local residents moving to shops by foot and by cycle but it's overrun with rat runners.
No bike lane, lots of parked vehicles, busy road.

There is no safe way to cross Forest Lake Boulevard to connect the bike paths on the northern and southern side along Blunder Creek. With a safe crossing in place, there would be a safe off-road cycling connection between the eastern and western portions of Forest Lake.
Slight up hill section slows riders as road narrows into intersection. Drivers will often attempt last minute overtake before performing a left hook in front of them into Carmody Rd.
Adding bike lanes for children to use to commute to school would be a big boost. A bidirectional separated lane would safest for young children.

Cyclists are banned from travelling to the port of Brisbane from this point, which means it is not possible to commute by bike if you work at the port.  The ban on cyclists was meant to be a temporary measure during construction, but the ban has never been lifted.
Cycle lane ends
Entry to/from bike paths to road poor. Just shoots riders onto the road (wrong side). Often in large groups, and 4 lanes to cross.
Just avoid this section due to narrow nature, volume and speed of traffic

This street is on the Principal Cycle Network, is uphill, and here there is a road deviation that obscures approaching traffic although following cars regularly overtake. It is necessary to either remove on-street parking (and make it a safe cycle street) or install a median so vehicles cannot overtake.

properties.Location: Howard Smith Wharves, Fortitude Valley, Queensland
properties.Description: HSW was designed as a place of consumption with the interests of cyclists put well into second place. This means a cyclist you're competing with people standing around, walking slowly, cars and so on. The boardwalk on the west side is especially chaotic during peak crowd times.

When traveling west and crossing the road from the dedicated bike path, a bike has to turn away from the roundabout that is only 20m away the crossing, and then it is very difficult to see if a car is turning off the roundabout towards you.
It is really hard to see oncoming traffic when crossing the road, and it is busy with fast cars on a corner

Technically, riders heading east after crossing Dean St are directed towards a one-way crossover/lane with riders heading west through the bus area.
To avoid this, riders cross on other parts of Dean St, including riding the wrong way along the east lane of Dean St.
During peak hour traffic this un-signaled crossing can be challenging and dangerous.
Bike lanes / shoulders in both directions are usually blocked by parked cars, so you'll be weaving in and out of traffic to say out of the door zone.
Pinch point as bikeway ends at footpath before hitting a crossing. Mix of cyclists, pedestrians and abandoned hire scooters....

properties.Location: 931 Kingsford Smith Drive, Eagle Farm, Queensland
properties.Description: Kingsford Smith Dr is one of the main arteries of Brisbane, and getting to the great cycleway up the road requires a few kms on a narrow, rough and very heavy traffic road
Caught in the middle of the road with limited opportunities to negotiate right turn if oncoming traffic is heavy.
The whole Queens Wharf is a mess - too narrow and mixing pedestrians and bicycle riders in an unsafe way

This area near the Pamphlett Bridge is on the River Loop and used by thousands every week. Almost every time I ride through here a car passes my bike right as the road narrows with a pedestrian refuge island in the middle of the road. The cars end up way too close.
The cycle lane to this location is nice and wide but as you enter the ramp up to the bridge it narrows and the light poles block the path. Totally unsuitable for younger or less confident riders.

There is a bikeway between New Farm Park & Teneriffe Ferry, and then one to Newstead Park, but 17 Skyring Terrace forces bike riders to compete with vehicle and pedestrian traffic. Furthermore I have found vehicles to be quite hostile along this stretch of road in Teneriffe.

Despite being small Worrell street gets a lot of traffic, many likely rat-running across the highway. A busy street that is part of the V1 should have nothing less than a fully separated bike lane. Currently it is only fit for confident cyclist. Measures to discourage rat running in the area would benefit cyclist and local residents too.
King Arthur Tce curves left here, cars very frequently cut the corner into the bike lane. I've almost been swiped here numerous times.
