Too often pedestrians are on this ride only lane, rather than the pedestrian lane on the otherwise of the bridge.
Difficult to navigate two lanes of cars to get to bike crossing

properties.Location: Fig Tree Island, Brisbane City, Queensland
properties.Description: The bikeway just ends here with no real connection to anything.
it's awful
Traffic along Sinnamon Rd is too fast and the slip lane is also dangerous. An intersection upgrade is badly needed here with a single set of lights to allow the full crossing.

Parked cars 24/7 here has meant the shoulder is effectively a parking lane, forcing cycling commuters to use the left-most lane in this contested stretch of Nudgee Road. Put in a no stopping line - public roads are for conveying traffic, not long-term private vehicle parking.

Shared footpath with what is supposed to be dedicated bike lane. Pedestrians running to the bus stop often in bicycle-only path. It's very narrow, there is 'road furniture' blocking entrance/exit, very easy for collisions to occur.

Regularly see drivers drive straight through this light-controlled pedestrian & bike crossing, completely oblivious to their red (stop) signal. Thankfully, not seen an actual collision - just many close calls.
The amount of traffic in this section always makes you feel uncomfortable whilst riding. As there is no cycling lanes or infrastructure, you have to ride in the car lane and slow traffic.

It is very difficult to enter or exit the Indooroopilly Riverwalk from Witton Rd. If you come from the ATC side, you can get trapped in the middle of the road. If you are exiting towards ATC, cars come around the corner with little time to react.
Road narrows suddenly on bridge crossing Cannery Creek. High volume of fast-moving heavy vehicle traffic on Nudgee road makes cyclists extremely vulnerable here.
Major choke point for active transport commuters that walk/ cycle/ e-wheel. Why can't there be another crossing to connect to the northbound lane of Racecourse Road and relieve this sole crossing?

During busy times, the Bicentennial Bikeway can be a dangerous place to casually ride when peleton riders are riding at speeds between 30-40kph.
Speed limits for cyclists need to be set on this bikeway.

Heading south the lane just ends due to ongoing construction works, and with no safe alternative in place.

Council needs to be held responsible for their inaction.

If you need to get in/out of Patrick Lane, the traffic light loops don't respond to a bicycle, so you either have to get over the pedestrian traffic lights on the wrong side of the street or hope that in some point a car appears and triggers the loop. This is also true for turning right into Patrick Lane coming dow Land St from Sylvan Rd

This is a single one roundabout but drivers use the bike lane as a left lane and have absolutely no consideration for cyclists! Often the footpath is safer but it is narrow and hard to share with pedestrians due to a fence and barrier on each side.  It's also not fair to pedestrians or cyclists!

This corner is very difficult for beginner riders such as kids to negotiate.  it is steep and has an awkward camber.  The whole bike crossing from Jack Speare Park is pretty awkward on a bike to be honest.  I suspect that this is why so many commuters don't use it but ride straight down Lambert rd.  Very high number of pedestrians in area as well.

properties.Location: 663 Kingsford Smith Drive, Eagle Farm, Queensland
properties.Description: Generally a good place to ride but often having to dodge lots of random debris in the cycle lane. Often see knifes, screws, bits of metal and rock debris from trucks. Occasionally also see drivers pull out into the bike lane to wait before entering traffic.
Slip lane is not "Safe System" compatible.  It should be removed.  TMR recommend against using them.
Dangerous intersection and shopping precinct for through bicycle traffic on Windmill St.

properties.Location: 59b Gardens Point Road, Brisbane City, Queensland
properties.Description: The new development here has ruined this section of once great bikeway. For such a high traffic bike area, segregated lanes away from pedestrians are needed. Otherwise this will end of like the south bank riverside path.
Major oversight - building a major riverside cycleway and rebuilding a ferry terminal that lacks a second intersection crossing for the increased footfall towards Racecourse Road...what a shame.

Merging of separate bike and pedestrian paths west of Gilbert Rd Bridge in to a narrow shared path causes accidents in very busy section of Kedron Brook. A continuation of an extra separate bike path needs to be constructed to continue on to the next footbridge at the AFL grounds.
Narrow bike lane, suddenly ends. Often blocked by vehicles.

properties.Location: 152a Mount Coot-Tha Road, Mount Coot-Tha, Queensland
properties.Description: Bike lane ends, and there's a pedestrian island for crossing the road, but difficult for bikes to turn from the left lane, across two lanes, and up onto the bike path that continues on the paved path leading past the botanical garden and over the highway.
One way intersections prevents school kids on bikes from crossing to Twigg st.  Where are they supposed to go?

properties.Location: Annerley Junior Soccer Club, Greenslopes, Queensland
properties.Description: Sharp 90* turns, barricade fence perfect for impaling, unclear signage, shared path with more popular for walkers than riders

This is a very narrow bridge on a high traffic shared path. It's not even wide enough for two bikes to pass. It also has sharp 90 deg entrances on either side. It's a big point of conflict along this route.

The bike lane from Latrobe terrace ends as the lane narrows, with parking starting suddenly and rounding a blind corner. These make it difficult to share the road, often with drivers oblivious to how close they pass.
Shared footpath with pedestrians. Very narrow area with no visibility near the bus stop. Dangerous for both pedestrians and cyclists.

Crossing here to travel westbound is not suitable for anyone other than the brave.  If we are to get more people cycling, we must provide comfortable infrastructure.  I'd not be comfortable with a 12 year old using this.
Traffic accelerating up hill, or spring downhill. This one of the main cycle routes from the northern suburbs. Speed limit should be 50, as it is for most of Herston Road.

The otherwise rather excellent Bulimba Creek bikeway is split here by a golf course. The detour involves a very large and steep hill that is partly on streets with absolutely no separation. This bikeway is very popular with families and young kids, and this detour would be a hard barrier that stops them from going any further.

All these reasons at once. The bike lane from Gladstone ends abruptly.
There's on-street parking even though these residences have big driveways/garages
It's a blind, high-speed corner intersection at Dornoch&Gladstone
It's a recipe for disaster headed toward West End on this road, but it's mercifully short.
Issues with cars pulling out from parking spaces, or from being parked in the bike lane without checking for cyclists.
No bicycle lane or footpath in Dryandra Rd underpass. Walking is banned by the Brisbane Airport Corporation who refused to include a footpath or bikepath in the design.

Cars are turning into the slip lane without stopping for pedestrian and cyclist crossing the road. A bicycle turning into Noble st does not have a proper insertion lane. It is dangerous as the cars are coming at high speed.

Part of the River Loop (back streets option), coming off very narrow bike path onto the street, cars careen around this corner and it's a sharp turn and difficult for cyclists to be able to check for turning vehicles. Highly trafficked by cyclists, needs sharrows.
No verge or bike lane on road and only one lane with double lines so cars can't pass safely.
A lot of traffic, difficult to turn right as road is very wide and doesn't have bike land
Increased (70km/h) speed limit on a uphill with zero shoulder.

high speed slip lane with obstructed visibility merging into high volume arterial road.  This is a high pedestrian and cyclist activity area.  A safe system approach would not include a high speed slip lane.  This left turn would be fully controlled and separated from vulnerable road users

Exiting the Sylvan Rd bike lane onto the separate cycle path is difficult because cars waiting to turn from Norwood St toward Milton Rd stop across the ends of the cycle path and don't leave enough space for bikes to pass between them.
There are many parked cars along Troughton Rd which block the majority of the bike lane and force cyclists into the traffic lane to avoid doors.
Drivers get too close to bikes on the curved sections
Poor visibility for cars coming over the overpass onto worrell st.

Ekibin Rd East in the main access point for Annerley / Tarragindi residents to the V1 bikeway, but there are no bike lanes. There is street parking on both sides, and the road has a 60k limit. It's very scary to ride there.
Have seen near misses where cars rat running overtake bikes on the blind crest and swerve back to avoid hitting traffic coming in other direction
No connecting infrastructure between Ipswich and Ipswich motorway cycleway. Brisbane road has several pinch points and cars parked on the verges forcing cyclists into heavy traffic
his is a critical link to the Enoggera Creek bikeway. On weekends, it is packed with people travelling to/from sport, with cars parked all over the place, and passing at unsafe distances.

There is a missing west-east link from Northern Bikeway to the Newstead Riverside bikeway. There is no safe bike travel path between the northern bikeway at Windsor Albion and the Teneriffe/Newstead reach of the river. You have to go over the dangerously narrow footpath on the Albion Bypass or make your way along very busy roads from the RBWH.

Signage on the (very busy) shared path puts onus pedestrians and cyclists to give way to turning vehicles, but legally turning vehicles should give way to path users. Davenport Dr is wide to allow for cars with trailers to turn, so it is also a long distance to cross. Needs a pedestrian crossing to improve safety.
If you hit this hole unexpectedly you could fall.  I suspect that carbon rims might not like it either.
Council has added squeeze points forcing cyclists into the main traffic stream.  I'd not be happy with my primary school aged child riding here ... so it can't be safe
Cars and trucks are often parked in the bike lane during the week which forces riders to use the car lane. This makes it dangerous when riding on this section of Sumners Road.
Bike lane ends unceremoniously right here, good luck with the traffic on these hills!
There is no safe or easy way to cross Boundary Road, although it is a key connection between two sections of the Centenary Cycleway.
Shoulder blocked by parked cars on a significant uphill (where cycling is slower)
The road narrows, forcing you into car lanes.

properties.Location: 168 Birdwood Road, Holland Park West, Queensland
properties.Description: Cycle path crosses roadway
The cycle lane often becomes submerged by salt water inundation at high tides. Cyclists have little warning and need to brake hard to avoid the hazard.
Busy section of ride with lots of bike and cars.  Would be better to have properr seperated lane here.

No safe connection between Durack Common and the new Archerfields Wetland precinct. A safe route could be constructed under (or over) the bridge on Blunder Rd at Hanley Creek, with a shared path around the edge of the council-owned Bowls Club and a safe crossing at Bowhill Road. Speed limits also need to be reduced here!

On street parking, lack of any dedicated/safe protected facility, the footpath is not useful for riding (no kerb ramps, and discontinous.  We ride here to get to the Newmarket pools. There is not direct link from the enogerra ck bikeway or up Enogerra Rd.  Drivers are impatient and makes my boys (9 and 12) very nervious.

No safe priority crossing treatment for people riding or walking on the western side of KG rd, or coming from Enogerra Ck bikeway up Hulmst to Bancroft to use the more SHADY side of Kelvin Grove road for walking/ride to school.   Speed of vehicles turning left,  ILLEGAL right turns from KGRd  (yes believe it), and speed of cars exiting Bancroft

A significant school children population on foot and on BICYCLES cross at Prospect Terrace crossing across KG Rd, to then cross at the zebra to the junior school. Drivers turning left do not give way or ensure children cross safely. Drivers observed doing mini -uturns around island on School St-LEstrange Tce to exit left here - makes this unsafe

Terrible crossing facility for riding to/from Pools or in general.  Slip lane faciities not adhered to by drivers, drivers/parents/people exiting POOLS area observed doing crazy manoeuvres to get around queuing traffic.  parked cars make it harder to see/navigate also.
Footpath is too narrow for cycling, many concealed driveways.
Bike and pedestrian lane changes once again and creates dangerous interaction between pedestrians and cyclists
High chance of car dooring. Car parking prioritised over cycling on a key link through the neighbourhood for commuters and recreational cyclists.

Turning right into Halsbury St is a common cyclist route to avoid Oxley Road. However due to the shape of the intersection, cars turning off Honour Ave, often at high speed, are very difficult to see due to the rail bridge.
Intersection requires several crossings of main roads - takes considerable time to cross at each point meaning many cyclists opt to ride in major traffic.

properties.Location: 305 Kelvin Grove Road, Kelvin Grove, Queensland
properties.Description: Drivers accessing the SHELL do not give way to, or look for people on the shared path at this point. Making this side of kelvin grove road unsafe for all ages and abilities.

After the bridge and down a concrete driveway you stop and wait for cars approaching from your right along Wharf St. You have to cross the lane of cars and turn right into a narrow green bottleneck bikeway (which is ok for a single bike) but when multiple bikes cross, the bottleneck quickly becomes unexpectedly apparent and dangerous.

Dedicated bike track passes directly in front of recycling plant. Track is often scattered with borken glass and sharp bits of metal, blocked by delivery vehicles parked on track while unloading. Track is also frequently crossed by forklifts and other industrial equipment.
Aggressive drivers, through main road.
Poor visibility due to trees
No cycle lane to go eastbound from Samford Ironbark MTB area. Westbound cycle lane on south side of Samford Rd is too narrow for two-way traffic.

As usual, cars take this slipw
 Lane way too fast and do not consider that people may be crossing. Considering there is a PCYC next to the slip lane and this is part of a bike path, a marked and highlighted zebra crossing is justified. (Dont bother with beg buttons)

This crossing is on a blind corner, meaning you more or less have to hope nobody is about to come around it when you choose to cross here (from west to east). This should be a raised crossing to ensure those less confident (e.g. those taking the motorway underpass route) can safely cross here.

Narrow footpath with bus stop on either side of Moggill Rd, bus passengers wait in middle of footpath. No way to cycle round them or past them (whether going east or west). No cycle lanes in road, traffic is too congested and aggressive to cycle on road.
Narrow footpaths makes it difficult to access Sunnybank Plaza from the west as this is the only viable connection (residential streets nearby don't connect up)
I see cyclists here at peak times when there are nearby bike paths. Even thought those bike paths are not as direct.

Travel from shared off-road path in park, on my outbound journey home to Carindale there's no easy way to get across this wide road at this bend towards Fryar St (I.e. back street cycling to avoid Old Cleveland Road). Cars cut the on-road cycle lane at speed, parents do illegal u-turn from child care centre.

Poorly designed intersection strongly  in favour of cars. Cyclists travelling eastbound on Stanley St cannot turn right when cars have a green right arrow, and instead have to wait to cross with pedestrians. This makes cyclists want to use main traffic lanes which is dangerous due to through traffic at intersection. Phasing also in favour of cars
because I do not trust cars or 4 wheel drives here to stop when I cross.
heavy vehicles cannot safely overtake a cyclist because of the cars parked on the kerb narrow the lane
No bike lane (East-bound) despite dedicated cycling traffic signal at Ann/Roma St intersection; footpaths are too narrow for bikes but so are the traffic/bus lanes!
Zero bike infrastructure along the super busy Stafford Rd. Footpaths are also very narrow and many ditches in the grass verge so it is hard to overtake pedestrians
Road has "audible" lane lines with very narrow shoulder.  Difficult to negotiate on bicycle
Footpath ends just before Jutland St
One of the busiest cycling routes into Brisbane city and the bike lane delivers you into a high hazard intersection.

Coolana Street is on the Moreton Bay Cycleway, so gets a lot of pedestrian and bicycle traffic. Site lines for footpath users on Coolana Street to the south along the Esplanade are very poor making the intersection dangerous for those crossing the Esplanade. A crossing should be installed from the north side of Coolana across the Esplanade.

Chelsea Rd intersection is offset to traffic. No cycling infrastructure. High speed limit and heavy traffic volume on New Cleveland Road. No safe place to wait when attempting to turn into London Road as the left lane (southbound) is used as a passing lane.

Poor connection into the community from Enogerra Ck cycleway -  lack of kerb ramps at intersection, visibility poor, speed of drivers, and lighting - lots of children use this route to access Bridge st Park, or the little Mountain bike track - and footpath itself is even not suitable for safe riding, and there is no on or off/exit bike ramp.

This section of Dillon Rd between Payne Rd and Highwood road is often pot-holed, and lacks a shoulder to ride on. It has become a major thoroughfare for workers (particularly in utes) who are rushing to/between jobs.
Bike lane is pretty narrow around this bend and it ends not too far away from here

Pick the lessor of two evils after you have crossed at the lights if you want to get down to the bike path. Choose to either go down an incredibly steep and narrow path or stay on the very narrow foot path and try not to get knocked into the path of the oncoming bus.

properties.Location: 16b Macgregor Street, Upper Mount Gravatt, Queensland
properties.Description: The only real link from the V1 to Westfields, very narrow footbridge basically means you'll have to walk the bike over
Low 10km speed limit means most cyclists wobble all over the place. Pedestrians wandering
Roundabout places riders in dangerous road position and makes them hard to see for drivers. Plenty of room here for a better dutch style roundabout.

After the roundabout the painted bike lane ends and then the road narrows into the pedestrian crossing. Forces riders to merge back with traffic while travelling slowly up hill. Aggressive drivers often try to quickly squeeze between rider and the crossing

This is the 'signed'  route (no treatment) to access Enogerra Creek bikeway for the community that lives near to the School (KGSC) and communities immediately south of Herston Road.    For children - the parked cars, especially between Dunsmore St and Rees St,  the speed of vehicles from Dunsmore St, and no continuous footpath
No bike lane, footpath too narrow to ride on
barrier directly orthogonal to the desire line is easily obscured by people walking and fellow cyclists.  It appears deliberately inconspicuous.
On road bike lane ends, forcing you into 70kph traffic. Bike path alongside is just a footpath with many blind driveway entrances.
Vehicles turning from Watson St onto Bennetts Rd have a poor sight line so encroach and block the bike lane.
Unsafe to get across here due to no crossing priority.

The on-road bike lanes end, and directs bicycle/e-scooter riders onto the path.... at a point where there is a street light post and it narrows where it would be difficult for 2-way traffic to safely pass
Very narrow two-way cycle path, pedestrians use this as a shortcut even thought it's marked as bike only. Difficult and dangerous to pass them especially when climbing up to the bridge.
Narrow path on steep hill with gate barriers at around halfway requiring very careful bike balance. Many riders dismount here.
Unsafe location as the road furniture is inadequate to actually slow traffic. Rather, it is taken like a chicane by drivers and acts as a pinch point bringing cars closer to cyclist.

properties.Location: St Peter Channell School, The Gap, Queensland
properties.Description: Bike lane is narrow and has a lot of leaf matter that is dangerous to ride on.

properties.Location: 406 Main Myrtletown Road, Brisbane Airport, Queensland
properties.Description: The footpath floods here at the slightest amount of rain, and the body of water does not drain for days/weeks. This forces cyclists onto the road with no shoulder amongst truck traffic.

The Rocklea bikeway goes through low-lying flood prone parkland, which is fine most of the time. Annoyingly for anyone coming from Salisbury along Tramore St, there is no ramp to connect to the path. You have to go "off-road"  to get from the street to the shared path.
Too much traffic as people use traffic light to turn right into Oxley Road.

Cars turning onto slip road to go north won't give way to cyclists trying to get to dedicated green tarmac bike lane at traffic lights. This means there's no safe way to cycle from Forest Lake to the Pallara State School or Sgt Dan Stller Memorial park
If you are cycling west to east, cycle lane ends at roundabout. No cycle lane or pavement on south side of Wadeville St, no safe way of crossing to north side
Cycle lane just stops, no safe way of getting from here to painted cycle lane at Stapylton Rd crossing
Dangerous section where riders are rolling down a hill relatively fast and coming off the path back onto the road.

properties.Location: Sir William Macgregor Drive, St Lucia, Queensland
properties.Description:
This road is part of the council's designated "Riverloop" and is a main connection between suburbs like Indooroopilly and the city. 

This whole stretch has cars parked on the left side, which are often arriving or leaving. Many are inexperienced drivers due to the road being part of the university. 

Risk of dooring is high.
No safe way of cycling to Pallara State School or the Sgt Dan Stiller Memorial park
Narrow and blind bend on the cycle path - some cyclists don't navigate it with care
Bike lane begins just before the corner and ends just after. Useless lane that moves riders into an unsafe position and forces them to merge quickly back into traffic. Might as well not be here.
surface to the left of the traffic island is extremely bumpy.  width to the right of the island is too narrow forcing cyclists too close to passing cars

The super-narrow off-road shared path has dozens of blind driveway crossings from here all the way to Lansvale St. It's safer to ride on the road, but then the aggressive drivers abuse you and swerve into the gutter to hit you for not using the path.

Postle Street is a reasonable alternate from the LGIP Primary Cycle route, Beaudesert Rd. Link Boniface to Desgrand, Kerry, to Postle St for a safe-ish route to Mortimer Rd at Acacia Ridge. Postle St itself can be problematic at peak times as it's narrow and used as a ratting route by car drivers. At other times it's better than Beaudesert Rd
Roundabouts are designed with speed in mind instead of safety. Drivers barely have to slow down to go around some of them
Too much traffic and no connection to underpass.
The shopping/cafe/train station section of Honour ave should be a max 40kph zone. Cars fly down here way too fast. Many such areas are now zoned differently, why not this one?

Banana bars, lack of path from woodland St,  poor visibility of the kerb ramp - makes this ENTRANCE to the bikeway a little unsafe. Lots of vehicles speed down from the nearby streets, turning left here from Woodland, and kids are crossing/accessing this space.   A slower speed environment, and making the entrance more visible is needed.
Cars never stop at the zebra crossing here, even though it is outside a school.
There is a lot of traffic on Simpson's road, and the traffic island is insufficiently wide to cross with a bike (wheels stick out either side of island).
Cars speeding through intersection, cars not giving way. Verbal abuse and road rage from drivers.
Lots of vehicles parked at this location narrows the cycleway and creates conflicts with pedestrians.
Cycle lane ends at an unsafe area (approaching an intersection uphill with parked cars) which creates a pinch point, especially with vehicles at or over the 60kmph speed limit.
Poor visibility crossing uncontrolled slip lane. Cars fly over the watlnd st overpass turning left, and solid concrete barrier and rise in overpass mean traffic can not be easily seen by cyclists.
Lane narrows at edge of intersection . Car taking off from lights often try to overtake around corner pushing cyclists into the curb.
Box trailer parked next to the kerb reduces the space available for cyclist to pass safely especially when there are cars passing on the right side of the cyclist.

In rain, and even 24hrs after rain, the adjacent pedestrian path is completely flooded (they then walk on the separated bicycle path, often without checking for approaching traffic) and the bicycle path is partly or completely flooded.
too narrow
Traffic intersection of Mary Street and Albert Street is narrow due to construction. Some drivers turning left into Albert Street from Mary Street do not indicate their intention.
Shared pathway on such a busy area creates conflicts between cyclists and pedestrians, particularly in the evening from bars and restaurants. The surface also gets very slippery when wet.
Bike path dumps onto street and then crosses busy road. Bike path continues through forest afterwards

properties.Location: 470 Upper Edward Street, Spring Hill, Queensland
properties.Description: All of Upper Edward Street is garbage for cycling. This is due to high volumes of commute traffic, little space for cycling.
Lots of debris accrues in northbound bike lane and it is very infrequently cleaned. Things can literally be there for months.
Square patch of the road that is a different level and causes tyre damage
The incline on the segment of footpath, between the road and the bicycle path is too steep. It is enough to throw a 700cc wheel, and becomes more perilous with a 20" wheel
Wild life and poor surface condition poor visibility
Road surface is really rough, especially on the edges/shoulder.
when there are lots of vehicles it feels unsafe to cross as you have to rush.
Reduce vehicle speed in this area that has a high volume of bikes and pedestrians

properties.Location: 42 Sir Fred Schonell Drive, St Lucia, Queensland
properties.Description: Far too high speed. Council wants us to go the Sandford St route, but the combination of parked cars, frequent turns, magpie, and extreme hills make that a poor option. Sir Fred needs to be made safer.

This should be a quiet backstreet that takes you to Nundah Village. Instead, it has ginormously wide junctions and no traffic calming, so people drive though at high speed. Given that it is the link between several nursing homes, a Qld Health facility, and Nundah Village, this is not safe.

There is a bike lane here to go straight ahead or left, but no road sensors to change the lights when only a bike is present. It is impossible to turn right from this intersection onto Beckett Road, it needs a bike 'pedestrian button' for the turn-right lane (car lane, no bike lane for this).
Very sharp blindspot turn. Risky if cyclists are coming in both directions at the same time.
Banana bars are very narrow, there is no angled on-path for bikes coming down Nemira St onto the bike paths. Pedestrians often stopped to chat right at path entrance, risk of collision is high.
The safety measures achieve the opposite because road rules are never enforced, better to forget it all and make it a straight road
Surface of the bridge detour is always covered in debris

Vehicles approaching Montague Rd blocking entrance to separated bikeway. Give way line needs to be set back and green bicycle lane painted to avoid collisions/near misses with cyclists entering/exiting bikeway from Montague Rd and vehicles approaching Montague Rd.

If you are on a bike trying to cross Sinnathamby Blvd onto Spring Mountain Blvd, traffic lights WILL NOT turn green as a bike isn't heavy enough for the pressure sensor. There is no button for cyclists to press to activate the lights - need to get off bike and press pedestrian crossing button
Signage asks you to ride on the footpath to go to a crossing for Long Street. The crossing is still blind to cars coming under the rail bridge.

When travelling upstream, the shared path crosses a bridge and then immediately turns right. The layout and vegetation make it very hard to see pedestrians or cyclists coming the other way. If you want to exit onto Akuna there is nowhere to stop and watch for traffic - you are either blocking the shared path, or already on the road

properties.Location: Springfield Greenbank Arterial, Springfield Lakes, Queensland
properties.Description: No safe way of crossing road between dirt cycle track leading south from Speckled Cct, and the Ironbark TRack on th south side of the arterial. Fast traffic in both directions and poor visibility.
No safe way of crossng Thonbill Dr, fast cars and por visbility as crossing point is at the bottom of a small valley
Cars entering and leaving Marist Brothers School don't give way to bicycles on Acacia Drive
No right-turn lane in the middle of the road maes it dangerous to get from northbound Yallambee Rd onto Capitol Dr. Southbound traffic is travelling fast.
This is where a bike path crosses a busy road.  The bike path comes steeply downhill to a road crossing where the cars have limited visibility and lots of distractions.
Blind corners and potential conflict with vulnerable pedestrians. A mirror would help prevent conflicts.

This corner is a 90 degrees turn shortly after the overpass - that daily probably 300+ people ride (weekends more). Whilst you slow down, if ANYONE is coming in the opposite direction [walker or rider] and a group is entering the corner you will have a fall. Badly designed path that could easily be fixed with some additional concrete.
Narrow and blind bend on the cycle path - some cyclists don't navigate it with care
The street is narrow with a bend that leaves cyclists vulnerable due to poor sight lines.
Lots of abusive drivers despite separate bike lane.
Avoid Nudgee road at all costs. Poor surface, poor drivers.

Council installed a large square of slippery grey paint across whole width of west-bound path (underneath the "go slow" marking). I was directly behind someone who fell at this location - they were not going quickly either.
Off camber round about, difficult to navigate especially given the downhill rolling speed.

The botched re-surfacing of the crit track is incredibly disappointing - and dangerous. Each morning hundreds of riders with OCD lap endlessly and funnel themselves through pinch point avoiding the lumps. The council contractor should have fixed this up - and/or fired the apprentice and supervisor who performed this travesty against bitumen.

no fun riding up hill in the door zone.

Not safe for kids.
left turn from Enerton St to Wilston Rd - "bike lane" on Wilston Rd should be widened to allow for cyclists to safely make the left turn without losing momentum

To carry on west down Moggill Rd, you have to cross Finney Road on a blind corner, walk a few paces south onto Usher St and then cross Usher St on a blind corner to get back to the Moggill Rd pavement.  When crossing, a cliff prevents you from seeing any traffic coming north along Usher St or west along Finney Rd
Too narrow for both cyclists and pedestrians
This could be a great connection to Racecourse road from the footpath on Kitchener Road. Unfortunately due to a very narrow footpath, the transition is rather unpleasant.

Cyclists along Josling st heading to the bicycle path on the opposite side of Brisbane st need to move into the slip lane to cross the road. Drivers will ignore give way signs and keep high speed through intersection.
There is lots of space to make the footpath wider on the bridge. Council instead try to fit two car lanes which creates further issues at the left slip lane ahead.
needs to allow cycling in the south-westerly direction

To connect up two un-connected sections of shared path, you have to ride Balham Rd (outside a Bunnings car park) and turn right into Marshall Rd. The visibility is low for oncoming cars, and there is potentially motor traffic behind you as you turn. It's a scary turn to make.
There's a reoccurring small but deep pothole here that will catch you out.
Bike lane ends in parked cars on fast descent and requires fast merge into traffic.

Very confusing, lack of direct safe pathways to access the Ashgrove Library when travelling from Stewart Rd / main Ashgrove Centre.  Does not make for an independent journey for children to go to library on their own.  parking in Amarina Ave Park, and illegible paths/crossing do not prioritise or make it easy to navigate for people walking/riding
Too much through traffic and visibility can be poor due to parking cars.
Bike lane does not connect to Kingsford Smith Drive / Australian Trade Coast precinct. This forces cyclists to use more dangerous routes such as Lomandra Dr / Sugarmill Rd or Nudgee Rd
Bike lane ends on a busy corner with no signage and lots of pedestrians.
Cars park across in front of the crossing point

properties.Location: 50 Lower River Terrace, South Brisbane, Queensland
properties.Description: the descent onto river terrace is steep and narrow with poor sightlines.  Sometimes there are pedestrians and people pushing scooters up the hill
Ferry access points just feeds pedestrians straight into a bike lane
Very narrow around the Riverstage with mixture of bikes and pedestrians and poor sight lines. Poor lighting at night

Carpeted area requires new surface. The carpet over the fibreglass grating is falling apart and lifting/folding in such a way as to cause a hazard to bicycles and definitely small wheeled devices like scooters.
Surface along Sylvan Rd is of poor quality
Main bicycle rout yet no safe infrastructure. Just a measly lane where care park adjacent

At the beginning of both ends of this protected cycle lane there are "Bikes only" signs, however, these are often not spotted by pedestrians, who take what they think is the natural path from the pavement before little dock st down to the parking area on dock st.  this can be a high speed cycle lane.
Vehicles turning left into Cribb St usually fail to give way to pedestrians and riders at this intersection.
Very short traffic light phase and is hard to get across both traffic lights on a bike
North Brisbane Bikeway (NBB) travels through Bowen Park. The path is already narrow. Pedestrians fail to keep left as signed.

The bike path changes from a marked seperate path with bikes on the east side of the path and pedestrians on the west side to a shared path with no separation and then back to separated after about 200m. Pedestrians spread across the path or even move to their left side causing potential collisions with cyclists in this area.

3 directions for both bikes and walkers entering and leaving make this very hard to feel safe. Walkers do not signal their intention and there is poor visibility between bikes from 2 of the 3 directions.

Cyclists are required to complete 4 separate signalised crossings to continue on the downfall creek bikeway. While waiting, shade and protection from fast-moving vehicles is extremely limited, and you'll be subjected to unsafe vehicle noise levels.
traffic sign poles clutter up and reduce the storage space for cyclists and pedestrians at this location
Cars are only paying attention to vehicle traffic and aren't looking out for cyclists

Left shoulder / fog line approaching Winstanley Street bridge ends on an inward curve. Bridge has no shoulder and far side of bridge also narrows further due to intersection and pedestrian crossing treatment. Left shoulder on approach to bridge is also frequently used by vehicles for car parking. Bus traffic.
The design of the bike lane allows cars to block the path when turning at the intersection
By law, bicycle riders and pedestrians have priority crossing this road, but there is confusing signage indicating that cars have priority

Weekend markets block the shared footpath through this area forcing cyclists to either dismount and walk through the markets, or take the road. Road is also heavily congested with pedestrian crossings and parked vehicles during this time.
I have seen many cyclists slip on this corner, due to wet surface or fine leaf litter
Awkward crossing here means you have to twist your neck to look behind you for traffic coming over the highway, and also watch right for traffic coming up Lakeside Drive.

properties.Location: 168 Mount Coot-Tha Road, Mount Coot-Tha, Queensland
properties.Description: Climb up this part of the hill is long and slow, bike lane is too narrow and drivers are too fast.
This section needs lighting. It's very dark and  lots of trees, animals so there can be debris day and night.

Shared bike lane / footpath is too narrow, not easy to give way to or pass pedestrians. Insufficient protection against fast moving traffic. Since this is the only way to get from Ferny Grove railway station to Samford Ironbark MTB area, there should be much better cycle lanes.
Waldheim St should have protected bike lanes. It's right outside Junction Park SS, which is a very active travel-focussed community. There is street parking on two sides, and plenty of space.
footpath ends part way down the old market site. Road is hostile so the only other option to avoid multiple road crossings is to ride on the grass

properties.Location: Victoria Bridge Abutment, South Brisbane, Queensland
properties.Description: No way to get to the southern side of victoria bridge from the Clem Jones Promenade without navigating pedestrian paths with multiple blind corners. Currently (17/01/24) construction has made this even worse.

properties.Location: 2 Chapel Hill Road, Chapel Hill, Queensland
properties.Description: If you use the pedestrian crossing, the footpath is too narrow for people going up and down the footpath, so someone has to step onto the road

properties.Location: New Farm Riverwalk, Fortitude Valley, Queensland
properties.Description: Better signage to encourage pedestrians onto the right side of the path would prevent issues

No on road infrastructure requiring cyclists to road in traffic (as shoulder often taken up with parked cars) or on footpath. But footpath has poorly designed segment around a pole with a sign sticking out in the way of people passing.
There is no ramp to access the bike lane from the footpath, so only someone who is confident to cycle along Garden Rd before the cycle lane begins will be able to use it.

awkward transition from Baradine St to Lind St in the northern direction.  In the southern direction the transition from Lind St to Baradine St involves crossing against the flow of traffic.  The eastern leg of the intersection should also have a crossing provided.
The bollards here make it difficult/annoying to get a bike through
Cars parked on the side road. Busy road. Most drivers leave at least 1 meter gap when over taking in pick time.

Heading from the bridge towards Annerley Rd requires far too much time spent waiting, including two full cycles to get across both legs of the crossing. Frequently see pedestrians and cyclists run the red light as a result. It should be possible to get from the west side of Gladstone Rd to Annerley Rd in under 1 minute maximum.
Blind corner that is difficult to cross going from south to north. New housing development means lots of new traffic too.

Cars do not obey stop sign when exiting Ebor Street. High traffic area with people going to the post office. Poor visibility. Cars turning into Ebor Street do not give way to cyclists/pedestrians. Needs built out kerbs, speed bumps, dedicated crossing and give way signs.

Pedestrian path here is ridiculously narrow, leading to the common situation of pedestrians choosing to be on the slightly wider (though still too narrow) bikeway. The pedestrian path needs to be doubled in width, and the bike path increased by at least a third.
Be careful crossing the lights here. Cars often run red lights coming to and from the bridge.
The surface of the bridge remains wet, long after rain has passed
Poor road surface along much of the section.
This stretch is uneven and constantly holed
Part of the River Loop. Needs Sharrows.
On ramp to cycle lane is after the bus stop area, putting cyclists back onto the road before they can join up with the cycle path.
the outbound transition from the bikeway to Logan Rd is indirect, crowded and has inadequate storage room

properties.Location: 180 Old Logan Road, Carole Park, Queensland
properties.Description: Ipswich City Council has installed a stupid barrier here which makes it impassable if you can't lift your bicycle.

A blind crest in the road, with insufficient width for cars to safely pass while remaining in the marked traffic lane. Despite this, many car drivers feel it is appropriate to overtake anyway (never mind you cannot see approaching vehicles). This is on the principal cycling network, and at best there are yellow bicycle symbols on the road
Footpath ends just before the road widens, creating a conflict point if drivers are coming in the other direction

Confusing intersection of footpaths and bike lanes - effectively a staggered crossroads with no give way signs. If you are going north and want to cross the brook it is unclear whether or not you have to give way to east-west pedestrians and bicycles.

Bicycle lane ends abruptly and funnels bikes out into a roundabout. Most cars don't know how to deal with bikes on roundabouts and it isn't large enough to share. Frequently have cars cutting me off and have had one collision with a car that tried to drive beside me on the roundabout.
When descending from Makerston St, slope is steep so it's hard to stay slow. Angle of junction makes it difficlt to twist head to look for westbound traffic on lower cycle path.
Path carries pedestrians and bikes on a slope, and is very narrow. It is slippery when wet.

Constantly experiencing drivers overtaking here directly beside me. No alternate footpath and no on road cycling infrastructure. Road generally quite wide but narrows in parts forcing cyclists to enter car lanes. Concerned that drivers won't give way to me when im required to enter in front of them.

properties.Location: 1873 Logan Road, Upper Mount Gravatt, Queensland
properties.Description: Drivers exiting this driveway onto Logan Rd tend to only look to their right, often driving onto the footpath in front of cyclist and pedestrians
This would be a great way for people to get around Kenmore/Chapel Hill on a bike, but it just stops at Moggill Rd with nowhere to cross or go anywhere else

properties.Location: 2 Barry Alexander Drive, Springfield Central, Queensland
properties.Description: Unclear where cyclists are supposed to be when going uphill towards roundabout

properties.Location: Mount Nebo Road, Enoggera Reservoir, Queensland
properties.Description: To get between Bellbird Grove and Enoggera Creek on dirt tracks, have to cross Mt Nebo Rd ere. Poor visibility and fast traffic, drivers don't expect to encounter cyclists.

properties.Location: 345 Pine Mountain Road, Mount Gravatt East, Queensland
properties.Description: Highly trafficked driveway exit/entrance to shopping centre. Vehicles frequently blocking/crossing the narrow footpath with poor visibility.

Cars approach fast, with very narrow gaps in the traffic and no pedestrian island. This is despite it being near major sports fields and parks, I think it should have bump outs or a pedestrian island, or something more than what it has.

Left stand-up lane heading northbound through intersection is frequently used by vehicles to rat-run/jump the traffic light queue at high speed (speed limit is 70km/h). Left lane ends/merges shortly after the intersection and shoulder is often used for car parking.

pathway here for cyclists riding north west on Enoggera Rd footpath is very narrow and steep.  pathway for people driving cars is smooth and gentle!  Try using a wheel chair here!  At the very least a kerb ramp close to the intersection should be provided.  A wombat crossing here would make for a smoother route
You can pick up quite a lot of speed here and the sightlines where the bend is aren't very good
pointless banana bars block the path.  The outbound transition to Baradine St is not smooth or coherent, and cyclists lose momentum

kerb ramp here is blocked by parked car.  Extend yellow line.  Kerb ramp is also too close to the intersection to be comfortable.  Suggest it is moved 20m further east and widened and angled to allow for smooth cycling transition to the footpath

A blind crest in the road, with insufficient width for cars to safely pass while remaining in the marked traffic lane. Despite this, many car drivers feel it is appropriate to overtake anyway (never mind you cannot see approaching vehicles). This is on the principal cycling network, and there is no bicycle markings of any kind.
Cars frequently park over the kerb cut onto the shared use path
Poor visibility from some heavily trafficked driveways along this section. Cars often parked across footpath.

Water comes up from the adjacent Cabbage Tree Creek when we have a storm or significant rain event.  Even after the water subsides, there is debris and mud left over which makes it slippery and unsafe.  Maybe nothing can be done here, but maybe there could be an alternative cycle path along Gympie Road over the bridge

The bikeway through Moorhen Flats is not lit and can be flooded, slippery, and uneven. The narrow pathway means that there can be dangerous conflicts with pedestrians, especially those walking dogs to the nearby dog park.

properties.Location: 1 Larwood Street, Upper Mount Gravatt, Queensland
properties.Description: Drivers going west out of Larwood St don't look to their right for anyone travelling south on Hoad St
Drivers of vehicles often run the red light here - even when the green walk light is given for the crossing. CHECK BOTH WAYS. Some drivers just drive straight through. Almost killed here.
Drivers of vehicles often run the red light here - even when the green walk light is given for the crossing. CHECK BOTH WAYS. Some drivers just drive straight through. Almost killed here.

The join of the path incline with the road incline is unsafe, particularly as bicycles are required to make a sharp turn (and if heading inbound would like to carry some speed to negotiate the hill on Fagan Rd). It is a problem for large 700cc wheels, and even more of an issue for 20" wheels
Deep drains on either side of the road, 70kmh speed limit around blind corner
Pedestrians, joggers and dog walkers use this cycle path as if it is a pavement and don't consider or give way to cyclists
Pedestrians, joggers and dog walkers use this cycle path as if it is a pavement and don't give way to cyclists
Conflict between pedestrians and cyclists when crossing bridge as bike path crosses pedestrian footpath. Pedestrians and dog walkers assume they have right of way and don't watch for cyclists.
Frequently get joggers with noise cancelling headphones who are in middle of bikeway and don't hear you sounding your bell

Bike lane ends on left side of Kitchener Rd, but the shoulder of the link rd is on the right (for parking) but is an awkward and dangerous transition. Plenty of room for road to be widened to accommodate parking lane, traffic lane and bike lane to link with Racecourse Rd.
Lights don't reliably change for bikes.  You either have to wait for a car, get off and push the pedestrian button, or just run the red.
Sharp, dangerous turn
When cycling from Ironbark Rd onto the Cubberla cycleway, you have to turn right but it's difficult to see traffic coming down Kirkdale Rd
Necessary to cross Highwood Rd, poor visibility
To get to Gold Creek basin, have to either cross Highwood Rd or cycle west and go around roundabout. Poor visibility.
Difficult to get from Bounty St onto Capitol Dr to access the Centenary Cycleway. Traffic coming from Endeavour St onto Yallambee Rd travelling aggressively and fast.
Risky junction whether turning left or right. Traffic coming from Lyndale Rd travelling too fast to be able to stop in time.
Shared path too narrow. Not enough space for two-way cycle traffic plus pedestrians and dog walkers. Some pedestrians intolerant of bicycles even though this is signosted as a bikeway.

Eastbound dirt path along the brook ends here, poor intersection with bike path - steep slope. Not clear how bikes should interact with pedestrians and who should give way to whom when crossing the bridge.
Kenmore South State School run a great program to encourage staff, parents, and students to use active transport, but there is little infrastructure to make this sustainable.
No bike lane or shoulder along a popular cycling stretch out to Cleveland Point. Always feels dangerous with cars passing closely and potential for doors opening.

This section was upgraded with automatic traffic lights however these don't work. A new button for cyclists to trigger the lights was added, however it was placed far off the path. This means bikes have to ride on the grass verge to activate the button, which drags dirt and gravel onto the ramp where bikes wait to cross.
Reasonably safe when not flooded - sharp bends from both directions need to be taken with care
Cars unaware
sharp turn, banana bars blocking the path and interfering with a smooth transition
Barrier is horizontal offset bars.  Unlike the one at the other end of this path, which recently replaced the previous horizontal offset bars.
pedestrians walking one way, and cyclists the other are forced into a narrower path than if the bars were removed.
The sudden stairway makes it difficult to safely use this route from Bardon Centre to Mt Coot-tha Botanic Gardens (and centenary cycleway)
This street use part of the Ennogera Creek Bikeway but is set up like a drag strip. It's wide, straight, 400 metres long and with a 50km/h limit. Some motorists see it as a challenge.
There is a total disrespect for all road rules at this spot. Caused by road rules not being enforced
Ramp up to the V1 from Stanley Street is too narrow and includes hard fences on each side of the entrance to the V1.

The bike path swaps from one side of Stanley St to the other at this point. In either direction, continuing along the bikeway requires two lengthy waits at traffic lights. Such is the inconvenience that many cyclists just dart out into the vehicle lanes to avoid the wait. 

Priority needs work.

properties.Location: 77a Lower River Terrace, South Brisbane, Queensland
properties.Description: Pedestrians crossing this section without looking
Access to Bicentennial Bikeway blocked for years
Tall set of stairs here with a track to bring up your bike
The crossing needs FULL chevron painting with 'DO NOT QUEUE'

This section on Dickson St is too narrow and the bike lane deviates in and out at the cross street intersections. It is easier to ride on the road, especially if there are bike riders going in both directions.
This path is too narrow to ride on. Better to use Vernon Tce if you want to get anywhere
Defeats the purpose of being off the road on a shared path if you can't each the pedestrian crossing button..

Missing a good link between the bikeway and the Victoria Park path. The paved one that was added would require riders to come nearly to a full stop and do a hairpin turn. It is currently safer to ride over the grass, albeit very bumpy.
Footbridge is too narrow.
Blind corner.  Pedestrian risk
Pole for overhear traffic lights blocks the inbound bike lane. Makes path too narrow for 2 bikes to pass safely without entering the pedestrian path
Drivers are supposed to give way to bikes here and the majority do, but I've had several occasions where I've nearly hit a car because they haven't stopped at the give way sign
A driver has turned into The Grosvenor without checking for cyclists, almost wiping me out.
If you want to avoid Fred Schonell Dr, you can go through here, but it is barely wide enough for walkers, and not wide enough for bikes also.
This "Bikeway" is a share path that is too narrow for walkers and bicycle riders.
Banana gate pinch point to be removed
Drainage here is poor and the path is often covered in water. There is also a separated pedestrian footpath, but it is overgrown, and walkers need to use the bikeway.

properties.Location: 21 Little Dock Street, South Brisbane, Queensland
properties.Description: Cyclists leave Little Dock St rather quickly, and I've seen one collide with a pedestrian here

low bollard is a strike hazard that can be obscured by others.  It has been recognised as a hazard as it has retro-reflective tape.  It should be removed.  Elimination comes above administrative controls, in the hierarchy of controls.
Awkward kink means you have to slow down quickly, poorly designed. Potential here for crashes.
banana bars unnecessarily narrow the pathway forcing pedestrians and cyclists to compete for space.  Please remove the banana bars
The surface heading north along here is quite rough. It could do with a re-surface
The entrance to the bike path from Taringa parade is always covered in loose gravel. It washes onto the bike path after rain from the unsealed parking areas.
concrete lane divider is a hazard.  if crossing from south to north when crowded it can be obscured by people in front of the cyclist

The bicycle path ends at the previous intersection but you can continue on the footpath for a while before having to get on the road. The footpath ends abruptly without warning with a drop off onto a gravel verge on the golf course side of the road. It is probably not particularly safe for pedestrians either!
There's a bump on the path and sometimes lots of leaf matter. I have lost traction going over the bump and injured myself.
The path floods when it rains and is usually covered in leaves. The council rarely maintains it and it's outside of the university boundaries.
Only suitable for a mountain bike - the path has potholes
Banana gate punch point should be removed
There's a gap in the network for recreational (family) cyclists between Deception Bay and Scarborough.
Entrance to Kooabura Trail from Crag Rd is in grvel layby, can contain broken glass, discarded cans and metal.

properties.Location: West End Riverside Lands Park, West End, Queensland
properties.Description: there is a traffic island in the middle of the cycleway ... absolutely no reason for it.
pedestrian crossing should be raised to allow for smoother cycling and slower car traffic

this low bollard is not visible till the last moment if you are approaching from the south west on the inbound footpath of Milton Rd and turning left into Frew Park.  Instead of putting some red tape on the bollard, council should remove it.
narrow footpath and poor surface

A bikeway is needed either on this side of the cricket fields or on the southern side to connect Kenna St with Maundrell Tce to allow people to continue on down Toomey St. This is to avoid needing to go along Ashley Rd or Hamilton Rd to reach Toomey St,.
There is no obvious way (well marked) to connect the Enoggera Creek Bikeway to the Ithaca Creek Bikeway. Please make a connection!
There is a small bike lane at the lights but it regularly ends up with gravel and other debris in it.
poor surface and ambiguous transition from Heroes Ave westbound to northbound bikepath

properties.Location: 324 Sir Fred Schonell Drive, St Lucia, Queensland
properties.Description: If you want to avoid Fred Schonell Dr, you can go through here, but it is barely wide enough for walkers, and not wide enough for bikes also.
traffic priority should be changed here to facilitate a smooth path for people cycling from Lind St to Ward St, not those driving cars
priority here should be changed to provide a smooth and coherent pathway for cyclists (not motorists)
totally pointless banana bars restrict the pathway
well worn desire line (as shown on the map!) needs paving
well worn desire line connecting the road to the bikepath needs paving
there is a well worn desire line that needs to be paved.  It connects Praed St to the bikeway eastbound.  Please have it paved.

Slip lane from Annerley Rd onto Cornwall St is extremely dangerous with poor visibility for drivers approaching from the north and turning left. Most drivers fail to give way to pedestrians and bikes here. The footpath is rideable but narrow with poor visibility.
The Bicentennial Bikeway crosses over Archer St at this location. I have almost been hit by cars turning from Coronation Dr into Archer St at high speed without indicating.

The on/off ramp to the Goodwill Bridge meets Stanley St and Sidon St at this location. Poor signage means that pedestrians, cyclists, scooter riders and occasionally cars tend to pile up here while figuring out where to go. People blocking the path, people cycling too fast for the conditions and piles of hire scooters all contribute to the hazard.
The Carl St crossing is unsafe, drivers frequently do not give way to bikes here.

Annerley Rd has seen an increase in fast vehicle traffic since the speed limit on Ipswich Rd was reduced. During peak hour this road is congested and is unsafe to cycle on. There is no cycling infrastructure here despite strong community support and many cyclists using this road.
Off road bike lane ends and you have to join traffic on the main road.

The South Bank Promenade is a lovely place to walk but not a safe place to cycle. It is a designated shared path, but has heavy pedestrian traffic, especially on afternoons and weekends. People step in front of bikes without looking. South Bank really needs a high speed dedicated bike path to reduce pedestrian/cyclist conflict on the waterfront.
Cars regularly ignore the right of way for bikes and drive through bikeway without looking. Be careful cycling through this intersection
Cars regularly ignore the right of way for bikes and drive through bikeway without looking. Be careful cycling through this intersection

The Edward St cycleway abruptly ends. Riders travelling northwest are forced onto the footpath here because it is a one way road. Getting to Central from here is scary. The bikeway should be extended to Wickham Tce to provide essential connectivity to Central Station, the Roma St precinct, and businesses in the northwestern half of the CBD.
The Woolloongabba Bikeway abruptly ends at this intersection and there is no safe route towards the east from here.

properties.Location: New Farm Riverwalk, Fortitude Valley, Queensland
properties.Description: Poorly designed transport link with significant conflict points between cyclists and patrons of Felons.
The turn angle of the path here is very sharp and the corner is blind. Pedestrians will often use the path here leading to crashes.
No bike lane
Boardwalk is very narrow and has tight corners / blind spots. Difficult to pass oncoming pedestrian traffic. Causeway can be impassable during king tide events.

Left turn onto freeway should have been closed as recommended when bikeway was built. Now only close for a short window peak hour. As a result bikes often dart across the traffic which is very dangerous
Have to come right out into the road to see if there are cars coming instead of seeing from the footpath
What was originally a shoulder beside a single lane has been turned into a dual lane that leaves no room for cycling commuters
Vehicles pulling in / out after dropping children at St Peters Lutheran College - very busy commuter route to Indooroopilly State High, Uni of Qld and City
Traffic comes around this corner quickly and there's a lot of cars at peak times. The bicycle lanes are discontinuous and end abruptly.

James Street needs bike lanes between Merthyr Road and Ann Street. The road is the only corridor linking New Farm and the shops at James Street. Car parking has been prioritised over bike lanes, and the new 40 km/h speed limit has not made much difference.

There is unsafe kerb line for cyclists to cross (at the end of road and start of Newfarm Riverwalk) that needs urgent attention. I expect the solution would not be very expensive but it will have major impacts considering the number of cyclists that use this bikeway every day. thanks. ( the kerb line needs a quick fix)

properties.Location: 214 Kenmore Road, Fig Tree Pocket, Queensland
properties.Description: Incredibly, Brisbane City Council have just spent $15 million "upgrading" this intersection and made it MORE dangerous for cycling by adding extra traffic lanes, turning lanes, and banning right turns into Pylara Street. Outrageous negligence from the engineers responsible.

properties.Location: UQ Lakes Bus Station, St Lucia, Queensland
properties.Description: This is an intersection of bikeways, there are no cars at this location. Bicycle traffic is heavy during peak hours and many people ride too fast for the conditions. This is a sharp corner with limited visibility and there is a risk of a collision between cyclists if people are not careful.

properties.Location: 170a Fig Tree Pocket Road, Indooroopilly, Queensland

The alternative route along Widdop Street forces riders to choose between riding in busy traffic in the door zone, or using a path crossed by driveways into the school carpark. A path here would also provide safe access to the Healthworks Gym.
There is absolutely no cycling infrastructure in Stafford.

The right hand turn from Gregory Terrace to Costin St is danger for cyclists who must sit stationary in traffic waitingto turn. The bike path running parallel to Gregory Terrace stops at Bowen Bridge Rd. Continuing along Gregory Terrace from there, decent cycling routes end. It's a reasonably popular commuting route for Fortitude Valley workers.
Horrible section of road, dreaded everytime I approach.
The Mt Crosby Road "camel humps" is the only link between Karana Downs and Anstead, but is extremely steep, and lots of traffic, extremely unsafe.
narrkw 0 9m old long single side path
People need to feel safe riding from/to the station and from/to the Carseldine Government Office Precinct and from/to Carseldine Central
Narrow road with no shoulder and 80Km speed limit (from here to Dayboro Road)
Bike lane only present across the intersection that moves riders into an unsafe position (too far left) where drivers entering struggle to see them.
Road narrows and bike markings on road end in parked cars. Requires merging with traffic up steep hill.

Aggressive higher speed traffic make it difficult to navigate this crossing in any direction across the intersection, even when dismounted and using the pedestrian crossing with island. Reduce traffic speeds needed along with separate cycle infrastructure.

Button to cross is too far from dip in kerb. Impossible to press with trailer on or with kid on bike. If you don't press it, you don't get to cross
very popular for both bikes and PEV area, can be improved and made safer by putting a physical separator from the road to make it legal for PEV use too
Terrible intersection to navigate heading through and up the hill

properties.Description: All of Caboolture River road heading west after Darley Drive. The speed is 80, it's hilly and winding. There's no space on the edge of the road to ride, and no footpaths. There's are work-arounds but they are long, involve walking, and are not much safer.
High speed section, no alternatives, high level of dangerous driving displayed, no protection or space given.

There is no bike lane or shoulder and a left hand lane going uphill here which ends shortly after the lights. It is frequented by exactly the sort of driver that will get angry at a cyclist who is unable to accelerate uphill at the same speed as a car. Road raged here multiple times despite having nowhere else to go.

Narrow bike lane only present across the intersection that moves riders into an unsafe position (too far left) where drivers exiting the half roundabout struggle to see them. The bike lane ends abruptly after the intersection.
No way to ride a bike easily from the shopping centre to turn right into Morayfield Rd.
